1	"""Citation health checks (M-17 — Phase C).
2	
3	Static integrity check over a loaded `AuditIR`. The audit bundle
4	exposed by M-16 only matters if its citation graph is internally
5	coherent: every `[#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]` token in the
6	verified report must resolve to a bibliography entry, every
7	bibliography entry should be referenced by at least one verified
8	sentence, and the bookkeeping fields (tier, span bounds, num,
9	evidence_id uniqueness) must be self-consistent.
10	
11	This module is the within-run companion to M-18 (regression
12	alerts, which is across-run). It does NOT fetch URLs or reach the
13	network — that surface is M-18 territory. Health checks here are
14	fast, deterministic, and run synchronously at audit-bundle export
15	time and on demand via the inspector endpoint.
16	
17	LAW II compliance: a broken reference is a real problem and must
18	surface as ERROR. We do not soften severity for "yellow" runs; the
19	operator decides whether a yellow audit ships.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import re
25	from dataclasses import dataclass
26	from enum import Enum
27	from typing import Mapping
28	
29	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.loader import (
30	    AuditIR,
31	    BibliographyEntry,
32	    VerifiedReport,
33	)
34	
35	
36	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
37	# Enumerations
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	
40	
41	class IssueSeverity(Enum):
42	    """Severity tiers for citation health issues."""
43	
44	    ERROR = "error"
45	    WARNING = "warning"
46	    INFO = "info"
47	
48	
49	class CitationIssueCode(Enum):
50	    """Stable issue codes for citation health findings.
51	
52	    Codes are stable strings (kebab-case via Enum value) so external
53	    consumers (regression-alerts dashboard, CSV export, automated
54	    triage) can filter without parsing message text.
55	    """
56	
57	    BROKEN_REF = "broken_ref"
58	    BROKEN_REPORT_CITATION = "broken_report_citation"
59	    INVALID_SPAN = "invalid_span"
60	    INVALID_TIER = "invalid_tier"
61	    DUPLICATE_EVIDENCE_ID = "duplicate_evidence_id"
62	    DUPLICATE_BIB_NUM = "duplicate_bib_num"
63	    NON_POSITIVE_BIB_NUM = "non_positive_bib_num"
64	    ORPHAN_EVIDENCE = "orphan_evidence"
65	    EMPTY_STATEMENT = "empty_statement"
66	    EMPTY_URL = "empty_url"
67	    VERIFIED_NO_TOKENS = "verified_no_tokens"
68	
69	
70	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
71	# Data classes
72	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	
74	
75	@dataclass(frozen=True)
76	class CitationHealthIssue:
77	    """One health-check finding, location-tagged for surfacing.
78	
79	    Optional location fields are populated only when relevant to the
80	    issue type (e.g. broken_ref carries claim_id + section_title;
81	    duplicate_bib_num carries bib_num).
82	    """
83	
84	    severity: IssueSeverity
85	    code: CitationIssueCode
86	    message: str
87	    evidence_id: str | None = None
88	    bib_num: int | None = None
89	    section_title: str | None = None
90	    claim_id: str | None = None
91	
92	
93	@dataclass(frozen=True)
94	class CitationHealthSummary:
95	    """Aggregate counts + an overall status traffic-light."""
96	
97	    total_evidence: int
98	    total_sentences_verified: int
99	    total_sentences_dropped: int
100	    error_count: int
101	    warning_count: int
102	    info_count: int
103	    overall_status: str  # "green" | "yellow" | "red"
104	
105	
106	@dataclass(frozen=True)
107	class CitationHealthReport:
108	    """Container surfaced to inspector router + audit-bundle export."""
109	
110	    issues: tuple[CitationHealthIssue, ...]
111	    summary: CitationHealthSummary
112	
113	
114	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
115	# Validation primitives
116	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
117	
118	
119	# Real V30 tier keys (matches run_diff convention). UNKNOWN is the
120	# explicit fallback used by the manifest for un-classified sources.
121	_VALID_TIERS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
122	    "T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5", "T6", "T7", "UNKNOWN",
123	})
124	
125	
126	def _check_bibliography(
127	    bibliography: tuple[BibliographyEntry, ...],
128	) -> tuple[list[CitationHealthIssue], dict[str, BibliographyEntry]]:
129	    """Scan bibliography for duplicates, invalid tiers, empty fields.
130	
131	    Returns (issues, evidence_id_index). The index maps evidence_id
132	    to the FIRST occurrence so caller can resolve token refs without
133	    re-scanning. Duplicates are reported but do not poison the index.
134	    """
135	    issues: list[CitationHealthIssue] = []
136	    evidence_id_index: dict[str, BibliographyEntry] = {}
137	    seen_nums: dict[int, BibliographyEntry] = {}
138	
139	    for entry in bibliography:
140	        if entry.evidence_id in evidence_id_index:
141	            issues.append(
142	                CitationHealthIssue(
143	                    severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
144	                    code=CitationIssueCode.DUPLICATE_EVIDENCE_ID,
145	                    message=(
146	                        f"evidence_id {entry.evidence_id!r} appears "
147	                        f"more than once in bibliography (first as "
148	                        f"[{evidence_id_index[entry.evidence_id].num}], "
149	                        f"again as [{entry.num}])"
150	                    ),
151	                    evidence_id=entry.evidence_id,
152	                    bib_num=entry.num,
153	                )
154	            )
155	        else:
156	            evidence_id_index[entry.evidence_id] = entry
157	
158	        if entry.num in seen_nums:
159	            issues.append(
160	                CitationHealthIssue(
161	                    severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
162	                    code=CitationIssueCode.DUPLICATE_BIB_NUM,
163	                    message=(
164	                        f"bibliography number [{entry.num}] is used "
165	                        f"by multiple entries (evidence_ids "
166	                        f"{seen_nums[entry.num].evidence_id!r} and "
167	                        f"{entry.evidence_id!r})"
168	                    ),
169	                    bib_num=entry.num,
170	                    evidence_id=entry.evidence_id,
171	                )
172	            )
173	        else:
174	            seen_nums[entry.num] = entry
175	
176	        if entry.num <= 0:
177	            issues.append(
178	                CitationHealthIssue(
179	                    severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
180	                    code=CitationIssueCode.NON_POSITIVE_BIB_NUM,
181	                    message=(
182	                        f"bibliography num must be a positive integer; "
183	                        f"got {entry.num} for evidence_id "
184	                        f"{entry.evidence_id!r}"
185	                    ),
186	                    bib_num=entry.num,
187	                    evidence_id=entry.evidence_id,
188	                )
189	            )
190	
191	        if not entry.statement or not entry.statement.strip():
192	            issues.append(
193	                CitationHealthIssue(
194	                    severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
195	                    code=CitationIssueCode.EMPTY_STATEMENT,
196	                    message=(
197	                        f"bibliography entry [{entry.num}] "
198	                        f"({entry.evidence_id!r}) has no statement; "
199	                        f"renderers cannot caption a citation without one"
200	                    ),
201	                    bib_num=entry.num,
202	                    evidence_id=entry.evidence_id,
203	                )
204	            )
205	
206	        if not entry.url or not entry.url.strip():
207	            issues.append(
208	                CitationHealthIssue(
209	                    severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
210	                    code=CitationIssueCode.EMPTY_URL,
211	                    message=(
212	                        f"bibliography entry [{entry.num}] "
213	                        f"({entry.evidence_id!r}) has no source URL; "
214	                        f"reviewers cannot follow the link to verify"
215	                    ),
216	                    bib_num=entry.num,
217	                    evidence_id=entry.evidence_id,
218	                )
219	            )
220	
221	        if entry.tier not in _VALID_TIERS:
222	            issues.append(
223	                CitationHealthIssue(
224	                    severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
225	                    code=CitationIssueCode.INVALID_TIER,
226	                    message=(
227	                        f"bibliography entry [{entry.num}] "
228	                        f"({entry.evidence_id!r}) has tier "
229	                        f"{entry.tier!r} which is not a valid V30 tier "
230	                        f"(expected one of: "
231	                        f"{', '.join(sorted(_VALID_TIERS))})"
232	                    ),
233	                    bib_num=entry.num,
234	                    evidence_id=entry.evidence_id,
235	                )
236	            )
237	
238	    return issues, evidence_id_index
239	
240	
241	def _check_verified_report(
242	    verified_report: VerifiedReport,
243	    evidence_id_index: Mapping[str, BibliographyEntry],
244	) -> tuple[list[CitationHealthIssue], set[str]]:
245	    """Scan verified-report sentences for broken refs + invalid spans.
246	
247	    Returns (issues, referenced_evidence_ids). Referenced ids are
248	    used by the caller to detect orphan bibliography entries.
249	    """
250	    issues: list[CitationHealthIssue] = []
251	    referenced: set[str] = set()
252	
253	    for section in verified_report.sections:
254	        for sentence in section.sentences:
255	            if not sentence.is_verified:
256	                # Dropped sentences may have any token shape; they
257	                # are not part of the rendered citation graph and
258	                # don't get health-checked.
259	                continue
260	
261	            if not sentence.tokens:
262	                issues.append(
263	                    CitationHealthIssue(
264	                        severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
265	                        code=CitationIssueCode.VERIFIED_NO_TOKENS,
266	                        message=(
267	                            f"sentence {sentence.claim_id!r} is marked "
268	                            f"verified but carries no evidence tokens; "
269	                            f"strict_verify should have dropped it"
270	                        ),
271	                        section_title=section.title,
272	                        claim_id=sentence.claim_id,
273	                    )
274	                )
275	                continue
276	
277	            for token in sentence.tokens:
278	                referenced.add(token.evidence_id)
279	
280	                if token.evidence_id not in evidence_id_index:
281	                    issues.append(
282	                        CitationHealthIssue(
283	                            severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
284	                            code=CitationIssueCode.BROKEN_REF,
285	                            message=(
286	                                f"sentence {sentence.claim_id!r} cites "
287	                                f"evidence_id {token.evidence_id!r} but "
288	                                f"no bibliography entry has that id"
289	                            ),
290	                            evidence_id=token.evidence_id,
291	                            section_title=section.title,
292	                            claim_id=sentence.claim_id,
293	                        )
294	                    )
295	
296	                if token.start < 0 or token.end <= token.start:
297	                    issues.append(
298	                        CitationHealthIssue(
299	                            severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
300	                            code=CitationIssueCode.INVALID_SPAN,
301	                            message=(
302	                                f"sentence {sentence.claim_id!r} cites "
303	                                f"evidence {token.evidence_id!r} with "
304	                                f"invalid span [{token.start}, "
305	                                f"{token.end}); start must be >= 0 and "
306	                                f"end must exceed start"
307	                            ),
308	                            evidence_id=token.evidence_id,
309	                            section_title=section.title,
310	                            claim_id=sentence.claim_id,
311	                        )
312	                    )
313	
314	    return issues, referenced
315	
316	
317	# Codex M-17 v1 review fix: report.md `[N]` citations are a
318	# first-class renderer contract — the inspector / audit-bundle
319	# delivery surface displays them, and customers expect [N] in the
320	# rendered prose to resolve to a bibliography entry. Bibliography
321	# nums are positive integers, never zero, and we only flag bare
322	# `[N]` patterns (not `[#ev:...]` provenance tokens, which are
323	# handled by _check_verified_report).
324	#
325	# We strip `[#ev:...]` first to avoid accidentally matching the
326	# integer inside an evidence span like `[#ev:ev_001:5-10]`, and
327	# we strip fenced code blocks so a markdown list "[1] Item" inside
328	# a code block doesn't surface as a broken citation.
329	_REPORT_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\]")
330	_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
331	_CODE_FENCE_RE = re.compile(r"```.*?```", re.DOTALL)
332	_INLINE_CODE_RE = re.compile(r"`[^`]*`")
333	
334	
335	def _check_report_citations(
336	    report_md: str,
337	    bibliography: tuple[BibliographyEntry, ...],
338	) -> list[CitationHealthIssue]:
339	    """Codex M-17 v1 review fix: report.md uses bare `[N]` markers
340	    to cite bibliography entries by number. Every `[N]` in the
341	    rendered prose must resolve to a bibliography entry with that
342	    num, or the renderer surfaces a dead link to the customer.
343	
344	    Provenance tokens (`[#ev:...]`), fenced code blocks, and inline
345	    backtick code spans are stripped before scanning to avoid false
346	    positives on integer-looking content that isn't a citation.
347	    """
348	    issues: list[CitationHealthIssue] = []
349	    if not report_md:
350	        return issues
351	
352	    cleaned = _CODE_FENCE_RE.sub("", report_md)
353	    cleaned = _INLINE_CODE_RE.sub("", cleaned)
354	    cleaned = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", cleaned)
355	
356	    bib_nums = {entry.num for entry in bibliography}
357	    seen: set[int] = set()
358	
359	    for match in _REPORT_CITATION_RE.finditer(cleaned):
360	        try:
361	            num = int(match.group(1))
362	        except ValueError:  # defensive — regex captures \d+ only
363	            continue
364	        if num in seen:
365	            continue
366	        seen.add(num)
367	        if num not in bib_nums:
368	            issues.append(
369	                CitationHealthIssue(
370	                    severity=IssueSeverity.ERROR,
371	                    code=CitationIssueCode.BROKEN_REPORT_CITATION,
372	                    message=(
373	                        f"report.md cites [{num}] but no bibliography "
374	                        f"entry has num={num}; the rendered prose "
375	                        f"shows a dead link to the customer"
376	                    ),
377	                    bib_num=num,
378	                )
379	            )
380	    return issues
381	
382	
383	def _check_orphans(
384	    bibliography: tuple[BibliographyEntry, ...],
385	    referenced_ids: set[str],
386	) -> list[CitationHealthIssue]:
387	    """Bibliography entries never cited by a verified sentence.
388	
389	    Reported as WARNING (not ERROR): an orphan source is allowed —
390	    the corpus may have provided it during retrieval but no
391	    verified sentence ended up needing it. Surfacing the warning
392	    lets reviewers see expensive-to-acquire sources that contributed
393	    nothing to the rendered report.
394	    """
395	    issues: list[CitationHealthIssue] = []
396	    for entry in bibliography:
397	        if entry.evidence_id not in referenced_ids:
398	            issues.append(
399	                CitationHealthIssue(
400	                    severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
401	                    code=CitationIssueCode.ORPHAN_EVIDENCE,
402	                    message=(
403	                        f"bibliography entry [{entry.num}] "
404	                        f"({entry.evidence_id!r}) is not cited by any "
405	                        f"verified sentence — the source contributed "
406	                        f"to retrieval but not to the rendered report"
407	                    ),
408	                    bib_num=entry.num,
409	                    evidence_id=entry.evidence_id,
410	                )
411	            )
412	    return issues
413	
414	
415	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
416	# Public API
417	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
418	
419	
420	def check_citation_health(ir: AuditIR) -> CitationHealthReport:
421	    """Run all integrity checks over a loaded AuditIR.
422	
423	    Pure function over the IR — does not mutate, does not fetch
424	    network, does not load source content. Safe to call from any
425	    request handler. Time complexity is O(B + S*T) where B is the
426	    bibliography size, S is the verified-sentence count, T is the
427	    average tokens-per-sentence (typically 1-3).
428	    """
429	    bib_issues, evidence_id_index = _check_bibliography(ir.bibliography)
430	    sent_issues, referenced_ids = _check_verified_report(
431	        ir.verified_report, evidence_id_index
432	    )
433	    orphan_issues = _check_orphans(ir.bibliography, referenced_ids)
434	    report_citation_issues = _check_report_citations(
435	        ir.report_md, ir.bibliography
436	    )
437	
438	    all_issues = tuple(
439	        bib_issues + sent_issues + orphan_issues + report_citation_issues
440	    )
441	
442	    err = sum(1 for i in all_issues if i.severity == IssueSeverity.ERROR)
443	    warn = sum(1 for i in all_issues if i.severity == IssueSeverity.WARNING)
444	    info = sum(1 for i in all_issues if i.severity == IssueSeverity.INFO)
445	
446	    if err > 0:
447	        status = "red"
448	    elif warn > 0:
449	        status = "yellow"
450	    else:
451	        status = "green"
452	
453	    summary = CitationHealthSummary(
454	        total_evidence=len(ir.bibliography),
455	        total_sentences_verified=ir.verified_report.sentences_verified,
456	        total_sentences_dropped=ir.verified_report.sentences_dropped,
457	        error_count=err,
458	        warning_count=warn,
459	        info_count=info,
460	        overall_status=status,
461	    )
462	    return CitationHealthReport(issues=all_issues, summary=summary)
463	
464	
465	def issue_to_dict(issue: CitationHealthIssue) -> dict:
466	    """Serialize an issue for JSON transport."""
467	    return {
468	        "severity": issue.severity.value,
469	        "code": issue.code.value,
470	        "message": issue.message,
471	        "evidence_id": issue.evidence_id,
472	        "bib_num": issue.bib_num,
473	        "section_title": issue.section_title,
474	        "claim_id": issue.claim_id,
475	    }
476	
477	
478	def report_to_dict(report: CitationHealthReport) -> dict:
479	    """Serialize a full health report for JSON transport."""
480	    return {
481	        "summary": {
482	            "total_evidence": report.summary.total_evidence,
483	            "total_sentences_verified": report.summary.total_sentences_verified,
484	            "total_sentences_dropped": report.summary.total_sentences_dropped,
485	            "error_count": report.summary.error_count,
486	            "warning_count": report.summary.warning_count,
487	            "info_count": report.summary.info_count,
488	            "overall_status": report.summary.overall_status,
489	        },
490	        "issues": [issue_to_dict(i) for i in report.issues],
491	    }
492

1	"""M-LIVE-2: extract competitor (ChatGPT DR / Gemini DR) prose
2	into the manifest shape that `beat_both_scoring.score_run()`
3	expects.
4	
5	The risk Codex flagged in FINAL_PLAN M-LIVE-2: extraction
6	normalization can invalidate the verdict. To mitigate:
7	  - extraction rules are explicit, deterministic, regex-based
8	  - per-dimension extraction is independent (one bad extractor
9	    doesn't poison other dimensions)
10	  - extracted manifests round-trip through the same `score_run`
11	    that scores POLARIS — no separate scoring path
12	
13	Public API:
14	    extract_competitor_manifest(text: str, source: str) -> dict
15	
16	Output dict shape (compatible with beat_both_scoring._get):
17	    {
18	        "citations": [{"url": str}, ...],
19	        "claims": [{"n": int|None, "baseline": float|None,
20	                    "endpoint": float|None, "ci": str|None,
21	                    "raw": str}, ...],
22	        "sections": [{"title": str}, ...],
23	        "tables": [],     # competitor docs rarely emit tables
24	        "report": {
25	            "narrative_word_count": int,
26	            "source": str,
27	        },
28	        "extraction_metadata": {
29	            "extractor_version": "v1",
30	            "source": str,
31	            "char_count": int,
32	            "word_count": int,
33	        },
34	    }
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import re
40	from typing import Any
41	
42	_EXTRACTOR_VERSION = "v1"
43	
44	
45	_URL_RE = re.compile(
46	    r"https?://[a-zA-Z0-9._\-/?=&%#~+:;,!$'()\[\]*]+",
47	    re.IGNORECASE,
48	)
49	
50	
51	_DOI_RE = re.compile(
52	    r"\b(?:doi:?\s*)?(10\.\d{4,9}/[\-._;()/:A-Z0-9]+)\b",
53	    re.IGNORECASE,
54	)
55	
56	
57	_PMID_RE = re.compile(
58	    r"\bpmid:?\s*(\d{6,9})\b",
59	    re.IGNORECASE,
60	)
61	
62	
63	# v2 R1 P1 #1 fix: v1 regex matched ANY short uppercase line,
64	# inflating section_count to 145 for ChatGPT (page-break residue
65	# fragments like "Trial", "Design and", "Sou" classified as
66	# sections). v2 requires Markdown-style heading prefix `#{1,4}`
67	# explicitly — far fewer false positives.
68	_SECTION_HEADER_RE = re.compile(
69	    r"^#{1,4}\s+(.+?)\s*$",
70	    re.MULTILINE,
71	)
72	
73	
74	_TABLE_RE = re.compile(
75	    r"^\|.+\|\s*$",
76	    re.MULTILINE,
77	)
78	
79	
80	_NUMERIC_CLAIM_RE = re.compile(
81	    r"(?P<measure>HbA1c|weight|body weight|BMI|systolic|diastolic|"
82	    r"LDL|HDL|cardiovascular|MACE)"
83	    r"[^.]{0,200}?"
84	    r"(?P<delta>[\-−]?\s*\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:%|kg|mg|mmol|mmHg))",
85	    re.IGNORECASE,
86	)
87	
88	
89	_CI_RE = re.compile(
90	    r"(?:95%?\s*CI|confidence interval)[:,\s]*"
91	    r"(?P<ci>[\[\(]?\s*[\-−]?\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:[,\-–to]+|to)\s*"
92	    r"[\-−]?\d+\.?\d*\s*[\]\)]?)",
93	    re.IGNORECASE,
94	)
95	
96	
97	_N_RE = re.compile(
98	    r"\b(?:n\s*=\s*|sample size of |included )"
99	    r"(?P<n>\d{2,6})\b",
100	    re.IGNORECASE,
101	)
102	
103	
104	_REGULATORY_HOSTS = {
105	    "fda.gov", "www.fda.gov", "accessdata.fda.gov",
106	    "ema.europa.eu", "www.ema.europa.eu",
107	    "health-products.canada.ca", "www.canada.ca",
108	    "nice.org.uk", "www.nice.org.uk",
109	    "clinicaltrials.gov", "www.clinicaltrials.gov",
110	    "pmda.go.jp", "www.pmda.go.jp",
111	    "tga.gov.au", "www.tga.gov.au",
112	    "mhra.gov.uk", "www.gov.uk",
113	}
114	
115	
116	_REGULATORY_TEXT_HINTS = (
117	    "FDA", "EMA", "Health Canada", "NICE", "PMDA", "TGA", "MHRA",
118	)
119	
120	
121	def _extract_citations(text: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
122	    """Extract URLs, DOIs, and PMIDs as citation objects."""
123	    cites: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
124	    seen: set[str] = set()
125	
126	    for m in _URL_RE.finditer(text):
127	        url = m.group(0).rstrip(".,;)")
128	        if url not in seen:
129	            seen.add(url)
130	            cites.append({"url": url, "kind": "url"})
131	
132	    for m in _DOI_RE.finditer(text):
133	        doi = m.group(1).rstrip(".,;)")
134	        url = f"https://doi.org/{doi}"
135	        if url not in seen:
136	            seen.add(url)
137	            cites.append({"url": url, "kind": "doi"})
138	
139	    for m in _PMID_RE.finditer(text):
140	        pmid = m.group(1)
141	        url = f"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/{pmid}/"
142	        if url not in seen:
143	            seen.add(url)
144	            cites.append({"url": url, "kind": "pmid"})
145	
146	    # v2 R1 P1 #4 fix: v1 injected synthetic regulatory proxy URLs
147	    # from plain-text mentions ("FDA", "EMA", etc.) into the same
148	    # citations bag that feeds unique_citations + regulatory_coverage
149	    # + jurisdictional_precision. One extractor error cross-poisoned
150	    # 3 verdicts. v2 drops the proxy injection: competitors that
151	    # don't cite regulatory URLs explicitly will (correctly) score
152	    # lower on regulatory_coverage. POLARIS's pipeline DOES cite
153	    # regulatory URLs explicitly, which is the right
154	    # comparative signal.
155	
156	    return cites
157	
158	
159	def _extract_sections(text: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
160	    sections: list[dict[str, str]] = []
161	    for m in _SECTION_HEADER_RE.finditer(text):
162	        title = (m.group(1) or m.group(2) or "").strip()
163	        if not title or len(title) > 80:
164	            continue
165	        if title.isdigit():
166	            continue
167	        sections.append({"title": title})
168	    return sections
169	
170	
171	def _extract_tables(text: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
172	    rows = _TABLE_RE.findall(text)
173	    if not rows:
174	        return []
175	    return [{"row_count": len(rows)}]
176	
177	
178	def _extract_claims(text: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
179	    """Extract numeric claims with optional N + CI annotations."""
180	    claims: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
181	    for m in _NUMERIC_CLAIM_RE.finditer(text):
182	        start = max(0, m.start() - 50)
183	        end = min(len(text), m.end() + 200)
184	        window = text[start:end]
185	        n_m = _N_RE.search(window)
186	        ci_m = _CI_RE.search(window)
187	        delta_str = m.group("delta").replace("−", "-").strip()
188	        delta_str = re.sub(r"\s+", "", delta_str)
189	        try:
190	            num_match = re.match(
191	                r"^([\-]?\d+\.?\d*)", delta_str,
192	            )
193	            value = float(num_match.group(1)) if num_match else None
194	        except (ValueError, AttributeError):
195	            value = None
196	        claims.append({
197	            "raw": m.group(0),
198	            "measure": m.group("measure"),
199	            "endpoint": value,
200	            "n": int(n_m.group("n")) if n_m else None,
201	            "ci": ci_m.group("ci").strip() if ci_m else None,
202	            "baseline": None,
203	        })
204	    return claims
205	
206	
207	def extract_competitor_manifest(
208	    text: str,
209	    *,
210	    source: str,
211	) -> dict[str, Any]:
212	    """Extract a competitor DR document into manifest dict shape.
213	
214	    Args:
215	        text: full prose text of the competitor DR
216	        source: human-readable label, e.g. "chatgpt_dr",
217	                "gemini_dr", "claude_dr"
218	
219	    Returns:
220	        dict compatible with `beat_both_scoring.score_run()`
221	    """
222	    if not isinstance(text, str):
223	        raise TypeError(f"text must be str, got {type(text).__name__}")
224	    if not isinstance(source, str) or not source.strip():
225	        raise ValueError("source must be non-empty str")
226	
227	    citations = _extract_citations(text)
228	    sections = _extract_sections(text)
229	    tables = _extract_tables(text)
230	    claims = _extract_claims(text)
231	
232	    word_count = len(re.findall(r"\b\w+\b", text))
233	
234	    # v2 R1 P1 #2 fix: M-D9 narrative_length / contradiction
235	    # scorers read `report.body`. v1 only set `narrative_word_count`,
236	    # forcing both scorers to 0. v2 also populates `report.body`
237	    # with the full prose so scorers compute meaningful values.
238	    return {
239	        "citations": citations,
240	        "claims": claims,
241	        "sections": sections,
242	        "tables": tables,
243	        "report": {
244	            "body": text,
245	            "narrative_word_count": word_count,
246	            "source": source,
247	        },
248	        "extraction_metadata": {
249	            "extractor_version": _EXTRACTOR_VERSION,
250	            "source": source,
251	            "char_count": len(text),
252	            "word_count": word_count,
253	            "citation_count": len(citations),
254	            "claim_count": len(claims),
255	            "section_count": len(sections),
256	            "table_count": len(tables),
257	        },
258	    }
259

1	"""Semi-automated contract drafting (M-26 — Phase C).
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN Phase C deliverable #3:
4	  Semi-automated contract drafting with mandatory human approval
5	  before customer-facing.
6	
7	The substrate that lets a Polaris audit produce a contract draft
8	(MSA, SoW, DPA, BAA) bound to specific verified findings, with
9	the explicit FINAL_PLAN guarantee that NO draft can become
10	customer-facing without human approval.
11	
12	Scope of v1:
13	  - Per-org draft registry. Each draft references a specific
14	    audit run + a list of clause_ids that the human approver
15	    must sign off on.
16	  - Lifecycle: DRAFT → AWAITING_APPROVAL → APPROVED | REJECTED.
17	    Approval requires a non-empty rationale plus the approver's
18	    user_id (so SOC2 audit later shows "alice@org approved
19	    contract X based on the audit_id Y at timestamp Z").
20	  - Append-only clause-decision log: each clause_id tracks
21	    individual approve/reject status. The contract as a whole
22	    can only flip to APPROVED when EVERY clause is approved.
23	  - Cross-tenant isolation throughout.
24	
25	Out of scope for v1:
26	  - Templating engine (DOCX, PDF). Drafts here are structured
27	    JSON (clause list + binding map); the renderer is a follow-up.
28	  - LLM-generated draft text. v1 stores operator-supplied
29	    clause text + the audit-run binding; the actual drafting
30	    LLM call lives outside this module so the audit_ir surface
31	    stays deterministic.
32	  - Counterparty-side workflows (countersign, redlines,
33	    versioning of negotiated changes).
34	
35	Codex M-26 v6 review (structural refactor): the prior v1..v5
36	parameterized helper `_transition_draft(to_state, from_states,
37	mark_decided, set_approver, set_rejecter)` had a combinatorial
38	bypass surface. Each round of review found a new (parameter,
39	value) tuple that escaped the invariants. v6 eliminates the
40	parameter surface entirely:
41	
42	  - Three concrete private helpers, one per legal transition:
43	        _perform_submit  : DRAFT → AWAITING_APPROVAL
44	        _perform_approve : AWAITING_APPROVAL → APPROVED
45	        _perform_reject  : AWAITING_APPROVAL → REJECTED
46	    Each is hardcoded for its specific edge — no `to_state`
47	    parameter, no `from_states` parameter, no bookkeeping flags.
48	    There is no parameter combination that can produce an illegal
49	    state because the parameters that would express "illegal
50	    state" do not exist.
51	
52	  - DB-level CHECK constraints encode the audit-trail invariants:
53	        DRAFT             : decision fields all NULL
54	        AWAITING_APPROVAL : decision fields all NULL
55	        APPROVED          : approved_by + decision_rationale +
56	                            decided_at all NOT NULL + non-empty,
57	                            rejected_by NULL
58	        REJECTED          : rejected_by + decision_rationale +
59	                            decided_at all NOT NULL + non-empty,
60	                            approved_by NULL
61	    Even direct SQL UPDATE attempts that violate these patterns
62	    fail at the SQL layer.
63	
64	Codex M-26 v7 review fix: v6 had three remaining bypasses:
65	  (a) `_perform_approve` / `_perform_reject` re-validated rationale
66	      with `.strip()` instead of `_sanitize_notes`, allowing
67	      content-empty rationales like "​" (zero-width space)
68	      to pass when called directly.
69	  (b) DB CHECK only enforced `IS NOT NULL` — direct SQL could
70	      write `decision_rationale=''` and pass.
71	  (c) DB CHECK could not express the cross-row invariant
72	      "all clauses approved before status='approved'", so
73	      direct SQL UPDATE writing canonical APPROVED metadata
74	      onto a draft with PENDING clauses was reachable.
75	
76	v7 closes all three:
77	  - Helpers route rationale through `_sanitize_notes` (strips
78	    Cc/Cf categories + whitespace; rejects content-empty input).
79	  - CHECK adds `length(field) > 0` to all NOT-NULL string fields.
80	  - SQL TRIGGERs encode cross-row invariants the CHECK cannot:
81	      * `trg_validate_status_transition`: closed transition table
82	        + all-clauses-approved + SOD enforced at SQL layer.
83	      * `trg_validate_status_on_insert`: new rows must start
84	        in 'draft' status.
85	      * `trg_freeze_clauses_on_terminal_*`: clauses are immutable
86	        after the parent draft reaches APPROVED / REJECTED.
87	
88	Codex M-26 v8 review fix: v7's freeze triggers covered UPDATE
89	and DELETE on contract_clauses but not INSERT. Direct SQL could
90	still INSERT a fresh `decision='pending'` clause onto an APPROVED
91	draft, which `assert_approved_for_send` then passed because it
92	only checks draft.status. v8 adds the symmetric BEFORE INSERT
93	trigger so terminal drafts have NO mutable clause surface.
94	
95	Codex M-26 v9 review fix: v8 had two more bypasses:
96	  (a) clause UPDATE freeze checked `NEW.draft_id` only — direct
97	      SQL `UPDATE contract_clauses SET draft_id = <non-terminal>`
98	      moved a clause OFF a terminal draft. v9 checks BOTH OLD and
99	      NEW draft_id so the trigger fires when either end touches
100	      a terminal draft.
101	  (b) `contract_drafts` row had no terminal-row freeze for non-
102	      status mutations. Direct SQL could mutate `title`,
103	      `counterparty_name`, `audit_run_id` etc. on an APPROVED row.
104	      v9 adds `trg_freeze_drafts_on_terminal` (BEFORE UPDATE) and
105	      `trg_freeze_drafts_delete_on_terminal` (BEFORE DELETE) so
106	      terminal rows are fully immutable.
107	
108	Codex M-26 v10 review fix: v9 had two more bypasses on the clause
109	substrate during AWAITING_APPROVAL:
110	  (a) Direct SQL `UPDATE contract_clauses SET decision='approved'`
111	      bypassed `decide_clause` and left no `decided_by` /
112	      `decided_at` audit metadata. v10 adds a CHECK on
113	      contract_clauses binding decision values to their canonical
114	      audit-trail metadata pattern.
115	  (b) Clause body/title/evidence_ids could be rewritten via
116	      direct SQL after a decision was recorded. The shipped
117	      approved contract could differ from what the clause
118	      reviewer signed off on. v10 adds `trg_clause_content_immutable`
119	      so body/title/evidence_ids/claim_ids are frozen from
120	      INSERT-time onward — only decision metadata can change.
121	
122	LAW VII compliance: stdlib only. Endpoints wired separately.
123	"""
124	
125	from __future__ import annotations
126	
127	import json
128	import sqlite3
129	import time
130	import uuid
131	import unicodedata
132	from dataclasses import dataclass
133	from enum import Enum
134	from pathlib import Path
135	from typing import Any
136	
137	
138	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
139	# Enumerations
140	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
141	
142	
143	class ContractDraftStatus(Enum):
144	    """Lifecycle states for a contract draft."""
145	
146	    DRAFT = "draft"  # Operator is still editing the clause set.
147	    AWAITING_APPROVAL = "awaiting_approval"  # Submitted; reviewer pending.
148	    APPROVED = "approved"  # Human approver signed off; ready to ship.
149	    REJECTED = "rejected"  # Reviewer rejected; cannot be sent.
150	
151	
152	class ClauseDecision(Enum):
153	    """Per-clause approval state."""
154	
155	    PENDING = "pending"
156	    APPROVED = "approved"
157	    REJECTED = "rejected"
158	
159	
160	class ContractKind(Enum):
161	    """Closed enum of contract types Polaris drafts.
162	
163	    MSA = Master Services Agreement
164	    SOW = Statement of Work
165	    DPA = Data Processing Addendum
166	    BAA = Business Associate Agreement (HIPAA)
167	    """
168	
169	    MSA = "msa"
170	    SOW = "sow"
171	    DPA = "dpa"
172	    BAA = "baa"
173	
174	
175	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
176	# Errors
177	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	
179	
180	class ContractDraftError(Exception):
181	    """Base error for contract-draft operations."""
182	
183	
184	class ContractDraftStateError(ContractDraftError):
185	    """Invalid input or state transition."""
186	
187	
188	class ContractApprovalGateError(ContractDraftError):
189	    """An attempt to ship/use a draft that has not been
190	    human-approved. The FINAL_PLAN's "mandatory human approval
191	    before customer-facing" gate."""
192	
193	
194	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
195	# Data classes
196	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
197	
198	
199	@dataclass(frozen=True)
200	class ContractClause:
201	    """One clause inside a draft.
202	
203	    `evidence_ids` and `claim_ids` carry the audit-IR back-links
204	    that ground this clause. A clause without back-links is
205	    allowed (e.g. boilerplate jurisdiction language) but the
206	    Inspector view will surface that as "unsourced" so the
207	    reviewer can confirm it's intentional.
208	    """
209	
210	    clause_id: str
211	    draft_id: str
212	    title: str
213	    body: str
214	    evidence_ids: tuple[str, ...]
215	    claim_ids: tuple[str, ...]
216	    decision: ClauseDecision
217	    decided_by: str | None
218	    decision_notes: str | None
219	    decided_at: float | None
220	    created_at: float
221	
222	
223	@dataclass(frozen=True)
224	class ContractDraft:
225	    """One contract draft.
226	
227	    `audit_run_id` is the V30 run this draft is anchored to.
228	    """
229	
230	    draft_id: str
231	    org_id: str
232	    workspace_id: str
233	    submitter_user_id: str
234	    audit_run_id: str
235	    kind: ContractKind
236	    title: str
237	    counterparty_name: str
238	    status: ContractDraftStatus
239	    approved_by: str | None
240	    rejected_by: str | None
241	    decision_rationale: str | None
242	    created_at: float
243	    updated_at: float
244	    decided_at: float | None
245	
246	
247	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
248	# Schema
249	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
250	
251	
252	# Codex M-26 v6 review fix: encode SOC2 audit-trail invariants as
253	# DB-level CHECK constraints. Even a direct SQL UPDATE that tries
254	# to write status='approved' with approved_by=NULL fails at the
255	# SQL layer. The Python helpers + the DB constraints form a
256	# defense-in-depth pair: the helpers ensure invariant maintenance
257	# during normal operation, and the constraints ensure that SQL-
258	# level mistakes (or future contributors who don't read the docs)
259	# cannot land an invalid row.
260	#
261	# Codex M-26 v7 review fix: v6 CHECK constraints only enforced
262	# `IS NOT NULL`, allowing direct SQL to write empty-string audit
263	# fields (e.g. approved_by='', decision_rationale=''). v7 adds
264	# `length() > 0` checks. Plus v7 adds three SQL TRIGGERs that
265	# enforce cross-row invariants the CHECK constraint cannot
266	# express:
267	#   - state-machine validity (closed transition table at SQL
268	#     layer): only DRAFT→AWAITING_APPROVAL, AWAITING_APPROVAL→
269	#     APPROVED, AWAITING_APPROVAL→REJECTED are legal
270	#   - all-clauses-approved before status='approved'
271	#   - SOD at SQL layer (approved_by != submitter_user_id)
272	#   - non-empty clauses before status='awaiting_approval'
273	#   - clauses immutable after terminal status (no UPDATE/DELETE
274	#     on contract_clauses when parent draft is APPROVED/REJECTED)
275	#   - new drafts must start in 'draft' status (no INSERT bypass)
276	_SCHEMA = """
277	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS contract_drafts (
278	    draft_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
279	    org_id TEXT NOT NULL,
280	    workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL,
281	    submitter_user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
282	    audit_run_id TEXT NOT NULL,
283	    kind TEXT NOT NULL,
284	    title TEXT NOT NULL,
285	    counterparty_name TEXT NOT NULL,
286	    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
287	    approved_by TEXT,
288	    rejected_by TEXT,
289	    decision_rationale TEXT,
290	    created_at REAL NOT NULL,
291	    updated_at REAL NOT NULL,
292	    decided_at REAL,
293	    CHECK (
294	        (status = 'draft'
295	            AND approved_by IS NULL
296	            AND rejected_by IS NULL
297	            AND decision_rationale IS NULL
298	            AND decided_at IS NULL)
299	        OR
300	        (status = 'awaiting_approval'
301	            AND approved_by IS NULL
302	            AND rejected_by IS NULL
303	            AND decision_rationale IS NULL
304	            AND decided_at IS NULL)
305	        OR
306	        (status = 'approved'
307	            AND approved_by IS NOT NULL
308	            AND length(approved_by) > 0
309	            AND rejected_by IS NULL
310	            AND decision_rationale IS NOT NULL
311	            AND length(decision_rationale) > 0
312	            AND decided_at IS NOT NULL)
313	        OR
314	        (status = 'rejected'
315	            AND approved_by IS NULL
316	            AND rejected_by IS NOT NULL
317	            AND length(rejected_by) > 0
318	            AND decision_rationale IS NOT NULL
319	            AND length(decision_rationale) > 0
320	            AND decided_at IS NOT NULL)
321	    )
322	);
323	
324	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_contract_drafts_org_status
325	    ON contract_drafts(org_id, status, updated_at DESC);
326	
327	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS contract_clauses (
328	    clause_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
329	    draft_id TEXT NOT NULL,
330	    title TEXT NOT NULL,
331	    body TEXT NOT NULL,
332	    evidence_ids_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
333	    claim_ids_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
334	    decision TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
335	    decided_by TEXT,
336	    decision_notes TEXT,
337	    decided_at REAL,
338	    created_at REAL NOT NULL,
339	    FOREIGN KEY (draft_id) REFERENCES contract_drafts(draft_id),
340	    -- Codex M-26 v10 review fix: clause-decision audit-trail
341	    -- invariants. Direct SQL UPDATE setting decision='approved'
342	    -- without decided_by + decided_at violated SOC2 in v9; the
343	    -- CHECK enforces the canonical pattern per decision value.
344	    CHECK (
345	        (decision = 'pending'
346	            AND decided_by IS NULL
347	            AND decision_notes IS NULL
348	            AND decided_at IS NULL)
349	        OR
350	        (decision = 'approved'
351	            AND decided_by IS NOT NULL
352	            AND length(decided_by) > 0
353	            AND decided_at IS NOT NULL)
354	        OR
355	        (decision = 'rejected'
356	            AND decided_by IS NOT NULL
357	            AND length(decided_by) > 0
358	            AND decided_at IS NOT NULL
359	            AND decision_notes IS NOT NULL
360	            AND length(decision_notes) > 0)
361	    )
362	);
363	
364	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_contract_clauses_draft
365	    ON contract_clauses(draft_id, created_at);
366	
367	-- Append-only decision audit log: every clause / draft state
368	-- change writes one row so SOC2 / customer-support flows can
369	-- show "who approved this clause and when?"
370	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS contract_decision_log (
371	    log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
372	    draft_id TEXT NOT NULL,
373	    clause_id TEXT,
374	    actor_user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
375	    from_state TEXT,
376	    to_state TEXT NOT NULL,
377	    rationale TEXT,
378	    created_at REAL NOT NULL,
379	    FOREIGN KEY (draft_id) REFERENCES contract_drafts(draft_id)
380	);
381	
382	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_contract_decision_log_draft
383	    ON contract_decision_log(draft_id, created_at);
384	
385	-- Codex M-26 v15 review fix (post-lock doc audit): the threat-model
386	-- doc claimed contract_decision_log was append-only "by design". It
387	-- wasn't — the table had no protective triggers, so direct SQL
388	-- could UPDATE row attribution or DELETE log entries entirely,
389	-- eliminating the audit trail that v12/v13 auto-log triggers
390	-- write. This trio of triggers makes the table truly append-only:
391	-- INSERTs only (existing auto-log triggers + create_draft).
392	-- No UPDATE, no DELETE, no truncation via DML.
393	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_decision_log_no_update
394	BEFORE UPDATE ON contract_decision_log
395	FOR EACH ROW
396	BEGIN
397	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'contract_decision_log is append-only; UPDATE is forbidden (every row is an immutable audit record)');
398	END;
399	
400	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_decision_log_no_delete
401	BEFORE DELETE ON contract_decision_log
402	FOR EACH ROW
403	BEGIN
404	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'contract_decision_log is append-only; DELETE is forbidden (the audit trail must survive)');
405	END;

407	-- Codex M-26 v17 review fix: the v16 PRAGMA approach only worked
408	-- for store-owned connections. A direct-SQL attacker opens their
409	-- own SQLite connection at the default `recursive_triggers = OFF`
410	-- and uses `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO contract_decision_log
411	-- (log_id, ...)` to delete-then-insert without firing the
412	-- BEFORE DELETE trigger.
413	--
414	-- This trigger catches the REPLACE attack at the BEFORE INSERT
415	-- phase (which DOES fire regardless of recursive_triggers state):
416	-- if NEW.log_id is non-NULL and a row with that log_id already
417	-- exists, it's an INSERT OR REPLACE and must abort.
418	--
419	-- Legitimate INSERT paths (auto-log triggers + create_draft) do
420	-- NOT specify log_id explicitly (it autoincrements), so the
421	-- WHEN clause doesn't fire on them. Forged INSERT OR REPLACE
422	-- with explicit log_id is the specific attack closed.
423	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_decision_log_no_replace
424	BEFORE INSERT ON contract_decision_log
425	FOR EACH ROW
426	WHEN NEW.log_id IS NOT NULL
427	     AND EXISTS (
428	         SELECT 1 FROM contract_decision_log WHERE log_id = NEW.log_id
429	     )
430	BEGIN
431	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'contract_decision_log is append-only; INSERT OR REPLACE on an existing log_id is forbidden (overwrites the audit record)');
432	END;

434	-- Codex M-26 v7 review fix: SQL triggers encode the cross-row
435	-- SOC2 invariants the CHECK constraint cannot express. Even a
436	-- direct SQL UPDATE that bypasses the Python helpers fails at
437	-- the SQL layer.
438	
439	-- Trigger 1: validate state-machine transitions at SQL level.
440	-- The closed transition table:
441	--   draft             -> awaiting_approval (with >=1 clause)
442	--   awaiting_approval -> approved (all clauses approved + SOD)
443	--   awaiting_approval -> rejected
444	-- All other (OLD.status, NEW.status) pairs are forbidden,
445	-- including any transition TO 'draft' (terminal-state revival).
446	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_validate_status_transition
447	BEFORE UPDATE OF status ON contract_drafts
448	FOR EACH ROW
449	WHEN NEW.status != OLD.status
450	BEGIN
451	    SELECT CASE
452	        WHEN NEW.status = 'draft'
453	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'illegal transition: draft cannot be a target state (no path back to draft)')
454	        WHEN NEW.status = 'awaiting_approval' AND OLD.status != 'draft'
455	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'illegal transition: awaiting_approval only reachable from draft')
456	        WHEN NEW.status = 'awaiting_approval' AND NOT EXISTS (
457	            SELECT 1 FROM contract_clauses WHERE draft_id = NEW.draft_id
458	        )
459	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot submit empty draft: at least one clause required')
460	        WHEN NEW.status = 'approved' AND OLD.status != 'awaiting_approval'
461	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'illegal transition: approved only reachable from awaiting_approval')
462	        WHEN NEW.status = 'approved' AND NOT EXISTS (
463	            SELECT 1 FROM contract_clauses WHERE draft_id = NEW.draft_id
464	        )
465	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot approve draft: no clauses')
466	        WHEN NEW.status = 'approved' AND EXISTS (
467	            SELECT 1 FROM contract_clauses
468	            WHERE draft_id = NEW.draft_id AND decision != 'approved'
469	        )
470	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot approve draft: at least one clause is not approved')
471	        WHEN NEW.status = 'approved' AND NEW.approved_by = NEW.submitter_user_id
472	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot approve own draft: separation of duties')
473	        WHEN NEW.status = 'rejected' AND OLD.status != 'awaiting_approval'
474	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'illegal transition: rejected only reachable from awaiting_approval')
475	        WHEN NEW.status NOT IN ('draft', 'awaiting_approval', 'approved', 'rejected')
476	            THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'illegal status value')
477	    END;
478	END;

480	-- Trigger 2: new contract drafts must start in 'draft' status.
481	-- Direct SQL INSERT cannot bypass the lifecycle by inserting a
482	-- row already in 'approved' (or any non-draft) status.
483	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_validate_status_on_insert
484	BEFORE INSERT ON contract_drafts
485	FOR EACH ROW
486	WHEN NEW.status != 'draft'
487	BEGIN
488	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'new contract drafts must be inserted in draft status');
489	END;

491	-- Trigger 3: clauses are frozen once the parent draft reaches a
492	-- terminal status (APPROVED / REJECTED). After approval, no one
493	-- can flip a clause from approved to rejected (or vice versa)
494	-- to retroactively change what was reviewed.
495	-- Codex M-26 v9 review fix: v8's WHEN clause only checked
496	-- NEW.draft_id, so `UPDATE contract_clauses SET draft_id = <non-
497	-- terminal>` could move a clause OFF a terminal draft (OLD points
498	-- at terminal but NEW doesn't, trigger doesn't fire). v9 checks
499	-- both OLD and NEW so the trigger fires if EITHER end of the
500	-- update touches a terminal draft.
501	--
502	-- This trigger blocks any UPDATE on terminal-parent clauses —
503	-- including legitimate-looking decision flips that would happen
504	-- post-approval. Decision changes during AWAITING_APPROVAL are
505	-- allowed (decide_clause); this fires only when parent is
506	-- already terminal.
507	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_freeze_clauses_on_terminal_update
508	BEFORE UPDATE ON contract_clauses
509	FOR EACH ROW
510	WHEN EXISTS (
511	    SELECT 1 FROM contract_drafts
512	    WHERE (draft_id = NEW.draft_id OR draft_id = OLD.draft_id)
513	      AND status IN ('approved', 'rejected')
514	)
515	BEGIN
516	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot modify clauses on a terminal draft (approved or rejected)');
517	END;

519	-- Codex M-26 v11 review fix: v10's freeze triggers only protected
520	-- the contract_clauses table when the parent draft was in a
521	-- terminal state (APPROVED/REJECTED). During the AWAITING_APPROVAL
522	-- window — between submit and final-approve — direct SQL could
523	-- still INSERT a forged 'decision=approved' clause, DELETE a
524	-- rejected clause, or move a rejected clause to another non-
525	-- terminal draft. approve_draft only validates the clauses
526	-- currently attached, so the bypass passed.
527	--
528	-- v11 expands the protection: clauses on a non-DRAFT parent
529	-- (i.e. submitted, approved, or rejected) cannot be inserted,
530	-- deleted, or moved. Decision-metadata UPDATEs during
531	-- AWAITING_APPROVAL remain allowed via decide_clause because
532	-- that path UPDATEs decision/decided_by/decided_at — never
533	-- draft_id, never the row's existence.
534	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_freeze_clauses_after_submit_delete
535	BEFORE DELETE ON contract_clauses
536	FOR EACH ROW
537	WHEN EXISTS (
538	    SELECT 1 FROM contract_drafts
539	    WHERE draft_id = OLD.draft_id
540	      AND status != 'draft'
541	)
542	BEGIN
543	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot delete clauses once parent draft is submitted (status != draft)');
544	END;

546	-- Codex M-26 v8 review fix: v7 froze terminal-draft clause UPDATE
547	-- and DELETE but not INSERT. v8 added the symmetric BEFORE INSERT
548	-- trigger to fully freeze clauses once the parent draft is
549	-- terminal: no UPDATE, no DELETE, no INSERT.
550	--
551	-- Codex M-26 v11 review fix: v8's INSERT trigger only blocked
552	-- terminal parents. During AWAITING_APPROVAL, direct SQL could
553	-- still INSERT a forged 'decision=approved' clause that satisfied
554	-- the v10 clause CHECK; approve_draft saw a fully-approved
555	-- clause set and let the draft transition to APPROVED. v11
556	-- expands the trigger to fire on any non-DRAFT parent.
557	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_freeze_clauses_after_submit_insert
558	BEFORE INSERT ON contract_clauses
559	FOR EACH ROW
560	WHEN EXISTS (
561	    SELECT 1 FROM contract_drafts
562	    WHERE draft_id = NEW.draft_id
563	      AND status != 'draft'
564	)
565	BEGIN
566	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot insert clauses once parent draft is submitted (status != draft)');
567	END;

569	-- Codex M-26 v13 review fix: new clauses must be inserted in
570	-- the PENDING decision state. The clause CHECK only validates
571	-- shape (decision='approved' with decided_by/decided_at is a
572	-- valid row), and the after-submit INSERT freeze only fires on
573	-- non-draft parents — so direct SQL could INSERT a pre-approved
574	-- clause into a DRAFT parent. submit_for_approval then sees
575	-- a clause set that's already all-approved and approve_draft
576	-- accepts it. v14 closes the gap by requiring INSERT-time
577	-- decision='pending'; decisions are recorded only via decide_clause.
578	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_clause_insert_must_be_pending
579	BEFORE INSERT ON contract_clauses
580	FOR EACH ROW
581	WHEN NEW.decision != 'pending'
582	BEGIN
583	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'new clauses must be inserted with decision=pending; decisions are recorded via decide_clause');
584	END;

586	-- Codex M-26 v11 review fix: clause draft_id moves are blocked
587	-- when EITHER OLD.draft_id or NEW.draft_id points at a non-DRAFT
588	-- parent. This closes the v10 bypass where direct SQL could move
589	-- a rejected clause off an AWAITING_APPROVAL parent into another
590	-- non-terminal draft.
591	--
592	-- The terminal-update trigger (above) already blocks all UPDATEs
593	-- on terminal-parent clauses including draft_id changes; this
594	-- trigger fills the AWAITING_APPROVAL gap. Decision-metadata
595	-- UPDATEs by decide_clause still pass because they don't change
596	-- draft_id.
597	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_lock_clause_draft_id_after_submit
598	BEFORE UPDATE OF draft_id ON contract_clauses
599	FOR EACH ROW
600	WHEN OLD.draft_id != NEW.draft_id
601	     AND EXISTS (
602	         SELECT 1 FROM contract_drafts
603	         WHERE (draft_id = OLD.draft_id OR draft_id = NEW.draft_id)
604	           AND status != 'draft'
605	     )
606	BEGIN
607	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot move clauses to or from a submitted contract (status != draft)');
608	END;
609	
610	-- Codex M-26 v9 review fix: v8 didn't freeze contract_drafts
611	-- rows after terminal status. Direct SQL could mutate `title`,
612	-- `counterparty_name`, `audit_run_id`, `kind` etc. on an APPROVED
613	-- draft and `assert_approved_for_send` still passed because it
614	-- only validates status. Terminal drafts must be FULLY frozen:
615	-- once a draft is APPROVED or REJECTED, no field on the row can
616	-- change. The transition trigger handles the status column path;
617	-- this trigger handles non-status mutation paths.
618	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_freeze_drafts_on_terminal
619	BEFORE UPDATE ON contract_drafts
620	FOR EACH ROW
621	WHEN OLD.status IN ('approved', 'rejected')
622	BEGIN
623	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot modify a terminal contract draft (approved or rejected) — terminal rows are immutable');
624	END;
625	
626	-- Symmetric: terminal drafts cannot be deleted either. Once
627	-- approved, the row is part of the SOC2 audit trail and cannot
628	-- be erased.
629	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_freeze_drafts_delete_on_terminal
630	BEFORE DELETE ON contract_drafts
631	FOR EACH ROW
632	WHEN OLD.status IN ('approved', 'rejected')
633	BEGIN
634	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot delete a terminal contract draft (approved or rejected)');
635	END;
636	
637	-- Codex M-26 v10 review fix: clause body/title/evidence are
638	-- IMMUTABLE after creation. v9 allowed direct SQL to rewrite a
639	-- decided clause's body/title between submit and final approval,
640	-- so the shipped contract differed from what the clause reviewer
641	-- signed off on. There is no public "edit clause body" API; once
642	-- a clause is added, only its decision metadata can change (via
643	-- decide_clause). Body/title/evidence_ids/claim_ids are frozen
644	-- from INSERT-time onward.
645	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_clause_content_immutable
646	BEFORE UPDATE ON contract_clauses
647	FOR EACH ROW
648	WHEN NEW.title != OLD.title
649	     OR NEW.body != OLD.body
650	     OR NEW.evidence_ids_json != OLD.evidence_ids_json
651	     OR NEW.claim_ids_json != OLD.claim_ids_json
652	BEGIN
653	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'clause title/body/evidence are immutable after creation — only decision metadata may change via decide_clause');
654	END;
655	
656	-- Codex M-26 v12 review fix: v11 still allowed direct SQL to
657	-- mutate clause decision fields without writing a corresponding
658	-- contract_decision_log row, so a forged decide_clause-equivalent
659	-- left no audit trail. The CHECK + content-immutability triggers
660	-- accepted canonical 'approved' metadata so approve_draft saw an
661	-- all-approved clause set even though no review actually happened.
662	--
663	-- v12 closes this with two triggers:
664	--
665	-- 1) trg_log_clause_decision_change (AFTER UPDATE): every clause
666	--    decision change writes a contract_decision_log row
667	--    automatically. Direct SQL bypassing decide_clause still
668	--    leaves an audit trail. The legitimate decide_clause path
669	--    drops its manual log INSERT to avoid duplicates — the
670	--    trigger is the single source of truth for decision-log
671	--    rows on clauses.
672	--
673	-- 2) trg_block_decision_metadata_drift (BEFORE UPDATE): blocks
674	--    UPDATEs that change decision metadata (decided_by /
675	--    decided_at / decision_notes) without changing decision
676	--    itself. Once a clause's decision is recorded, its
677	--    attribution is immutable — only re-deciding via decide_clause
678	--    can change who/when/why.
679	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_log_clause_decision_change
680	AFTER UPDATE ON contract_clauses
681	FOR EACH ROW
682	WHEN OLD.decision != NEW.decision
683	BEGIN
684	    INSERT INTO contract_decision_log (
685	        draft_id, clause_id, actor_user_id,
686	        from_state, to_state, rationale, created_at
687	    ) VALUES (
688	        NEW.draft_id, NEW.clause_id, NEW.decided_by,
689	        OLD.decision, NEW.decision, NEW.decision_notes,
690	        NEW.decided_at
691	    );
692	END;
693	
694	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_block_decision_metadata_drift
695	BEFORE UPDATE ON contract_clauses
696	FOR EACH ROW
697	WHEN OLD.decision = NEW.decision
698	     AND (
699	         (OLD.decided_by IS NOT NEW.decided_by
700	              AND (OLD.decided_by IS NULL OR NEW.decided_by IS NULL
701	                   OR OLD.decided_by != NEW.decided_by))
702	         OR (OLD.decided_at IS NOT NEW.decided_at
703	              AND (OLD.decided_at IS NULL OR NEW.decided_at IS NULL
704	                   OR OLD.decided_at != NEW.decided_at))
705	         OR (OLD.decision_notes IS NOT NEW.decision_notes
706	              AND (OLD.decision_notes IS NULL
707	                   OR NEW.decision_notes IS NULL
708	                   OR OLD.decision_notes != NEW.decision_notes))
709	     )
710	BEGIN
711	    SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'cannot mutate clause decision metadata without a decision change — re-decide via decide_clause to retag attribution');
712	END;
713	
714	-- Codex M-26 v13 review fix: symmetric to v12's clause auto-log.
715	-- v12 closed the audit-trail gap on contract_clauses but left
716	-- contract_drafts unprotected — direct SQL UPDATE flipping
717	-- draft status (from awaiting_approval to approved with valid
718	-- CHECK fields + non-self approver) bypassed `_perform_*`
719	-- helpers and left no contract_decision_log row for the
720	-- transition. v13 auto-logs all status transitions on drafts.
721	--
722	-- The legitimate `_perform_submit` / `_perform_approve` /
723	-- `_perform_reject` helpers drop their manual log INSERT (the
724	-- trigger is now the single source of truth for status-change
725	-- log rows). create_draft's "created" log entry is independent
726	-- (an INSERT, not an UPDATE) and stays.
727	--
728	-- Actor selection logic mirrors the legitimate flow:
729	--   awaiting_approval target  -> submitter_user_id
730	--   approved target           -> approved_by (set in same UPDATE)
731	--   rejected target           -> rejected_by (set in same UPDATE)
732	CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_log_draft_status_change
733	AFTER UPDATE OF status ON contract_drafts
734	FOR EACH ROW
735	WHEN OLD.status != NEW.status
736	BEGIN
737	    INSERT INTO contract_decision_log (
738	        draft_id, clause_id, actor_user_id,
739	        from_state, to_state, rationale, created_at
740	    ) VALUES (
741	        NEW.draft_id, NULL,
742	        CASE
743	            WHEN NEW.status = 'awaiting_approval'
744	                THEN NEW.submitter_user_id
745	            WHEN NEW.status = 'approved'
746	                THEN NEW.approved_by
747	            WHEN NEW.status = 'rejected'
748	                THEN NEW.rejected_by
749	            ELSE NEW.submitter_user_id
750	        END,
751	        OLD.status, NEW.status,
752	        NEW.decision_rationale,
753	        NEW.updated_at
754	    );
755	END;

763	
764	class ContractDraftStore:
765	    """SQLite-backed contract-draft registry.
766	
767	    Authorization posture: the store enforces cross-tenant
768	    isolation (every read/write is org-scoped), but does NOT
769	    validate role. The endpoint layer must gate on owner/admin
770	    role for `submit_for_approval` / `approve_draft` /
771	    `reject_draft` so a regular member cannot self-approve their
772	    own contract drafts.
773	
774	    Codex M-26 v6 review (structural refactor): the lifecycle
775	    transition surface is now three hardcoded private helpers
776	    (`_perform_submit`, `_perform_approve`, `_perform_reject`),
777	    one per legal edge. There is no parameterized helper that
778	    could be invoked with a malicious (to_state, from_states,
779	    mark_decided, set_approver, set_rejecter) tuple. Direct
780	    callers reaching for the underscore-prefixed methods STILL
781	    cannot violate state-machine invariants, because the legal-
782	    edge enforcement is hardcoded into each helper rather than
783	    being a runtime parameter check.
784	    """
785	
786	    def __init__(self, db_path: Path) -> None:
787	        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
788	        self._db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
789	        self._init_schema()
790	
791	    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
792	        conn = sqlite3.connect(
793	            self._db_path, isolation_level=None, timeout=30.0,
794	        )
795	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
796	        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
797	        conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
798	        # Codex M-26 v16 review fix: with `recursive_triggers` OFF
799	        # (SQLite default), `INSERT OR REPLACE` / `REPLACE INTO`
800	        # deletes + reinserts WITHOUT firing BEFORE DELETE triggers.

800	        # deletes + reinserts WITHOUT firing BEFORE DELETE triggers.
801	        # That bypassed v15's `trg_decision_log_no_delete` and let
802	        # an attacker rewrite forged log rows via REPLACE. Turning
803	        # the PRAGMA ON makes the implicit DELETE phase fire the
804	        # trigger, closing the v15-on-v15 bypass.
805	        conn.execute("PRAGMA recursive_triggers = ON")
806	        return conn
807	
808	    def _init_schema(self) -> None:
809	        with self._connect() as conn:
810	            conn.executescript(_SCHEMA)

816	    def create_draft(
817	        self,
818	        *,
819	        org_id: str,
820	        workspace_id: str,
821	        submitter_user_id: str,
822	        audit_run_id: str,
823	        kind: ContractKind,
824	        title: str,
825	        counterparty_name: str,
826	    ) -> ContractDraft:
827	        if not org_id.strip() or not workspace_id.strip():
828	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
829	                "org_id and workspace_id must be non-empty"
830	            )
831	        if not submitter_user_id.strip():
832	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
833	                "submitter_user_id must be non-empty"
834	            )
835	        if not audit_run_id.strip():
836	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
837	                "audit_run_id must be non-empty — every contract "
838	                "draft must be anchored to a specific verified "
839	                "audit run"
840	            )
841	        if not isinstance(kind, ContractKind):
842	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
843	                f"kind must be ContractKind, got {kind!r}"
844	            )
845	        if not title.strip():
846	            raise ContractDraftStateError("title must be non-empty")
847	        if not counterparty_name.strip():
848	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
849	                "counterparty_name must be non-empty"
850	            )
851	        draft_id = f"ctr_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
852	        now = time.time()
853	        with self._connect() as conn:
854	            conn.execute(
855	                "INSERT INTO contract_drafts (draft_id, org_id, "
856	                "workspace_id, submitter_user_id, audit_run_id, "
857	                "kind, title, counterparty_name, status, created_at, "
858	                "updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
859	                (
860	                    draft_id, org_id.strip(), workspace_id.strip(),
861	                    submitter_user_id.strip(), audit_run_id.strip(),
862	                    kind.value, title.strip(),
863	                    counterparty_name.strip(),
864	                    ContractDraftStatus.DRAFT.value, now, now,
865	                ),
866	            )
867	            conn.execute(
868	                "INSERT INTO contract_decision_log (draft_id, "
869	                "clause_id, actor_user_id, from_state, to_state, "
870	                "rationale, created_at) VALUES "
871	                "(?, NULL, ?, NULL, ?, ?, ?)",
872	                (
873	                    draft_id, submitter_user_id.strip(),
874	                    ContractDraftStatus.DRAFT.value,
875	                    "created", now,
876	                ),
877	            )
878	        return ContractDraft(
879	            draft_id=draft_id, org_id=org_id.strip(),
880	            workspace_id=workspace_id.strip(),
881	            submitter_user_id=submitter_user_id.strip(),
882	            audit_run_id=audit_run_id.strip(),
883	            kind=kind, title=title.strip(),
884	            counterparty_name=counterparty_name.strip(),
885	            status=ContractDraftStatus.DRAFT,
886	            approved_by=None, rejected_by=None,
887	            decision_rationale=None,
888	            created_at=now, updated_at=now, decided_at=None,
889	        )

895	    def add_clause(
896	        self,
897	        *,
898	        draft_id: str,
899	        org_id: str,
900	        title: str,
901	        body: str,
902	        evidence_ids: tuple[str, ...] = (),
903	        claim_ids: tuple[str, ...] = (),
904	    ) -> ContractClause:
905	        if not title.strip() or not body.strip():
906	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
907	                "clause title and body must be non-empty"
908	            )
909	        # Verify draft exists in this org and is still editable.
910	        draft = self.get_draft(draft_id=draft_id, org_id=org_id)
911	        if draft is None:
912	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
913	                f"draft {draft_id!r} is not accessible to this caller"
914	            )
915	        if draft.status != ContractDraftStatus.DRAFT:
916	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
917	                f"draft {draft_id!r} is in state {draft.status.value!r}; "
918	                f"clauses can only be added while in DRAFT state"
919	            )
920	        clause_id = f"cls_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
921	        now = time.time()
922	        with self._connect() as conn:
923	            conn.execute(
924	                "INSERT INTO contract_clauses (clause_id, draft_id, "
925	                "title, body, evidence_ids_json, claim_ids_json, "
926	                "decision, created_at) VALUES "
927	                "(?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
928	                (
929	                    clause_id, draft_id, title.strip(), body.strip(),
930	                    json.dumps(list(evidence_ids), sort_keys=True),
931	                    json.dumps(list(claim_ids), sort_keys=True),

933	                ),

937	                "WHERE draft_id = ?",

951	        self, *, draft_id: str, org_id: str,
952	    ) -> list[ContractClause]:
953	        if self.get_draft(draft_id=draft_id, org_id=org_id) is None:
954	            return []
955	        with self._connect() as conn:
956	            rows = conn.execute(
957	                "SELECT * FROM contract_clauses WHERE draft_id = ? "

963	    def decide_clause(
964	        self,
965	        *,
966	        clause_id: str,
967	        org_id: str,
968	        approver_user_id: str,
969	        decision: ClauseDecision,
970	        notes: str | None = None,
971	    ) -> ContractClause:
972	        """Per-clause approve / reject. Per-clause decisions are
973	        durable and auditable; the overall draft only flips to
974	        APPROVED when EVERY clause is APPROVED."""
975	        if not approver_user_id.strip():
976	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
977	                "approver_user_id must be non-empty"
978	            )
979	        if decision == ClauseDecision.PENDING:
980	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
981	                "cannot decide a clause as PENDING; pass APPROVED "
982	                "or REJECTED"
983	            )
984	        sanitized_notes = _sanitize_notes(notes)
985	        if decision == ClauseDecision.REJECTED and sanitized_notes is None:
986	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
987	                "rejecting a clause requires non-empty notes"
988	            )
989	        now = time.time()
990	        with self._connect() as conn:
991	            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
992	            try:
993	                clause_row = conn.execute(
994	                    "SELECT c.*, d.status AS draft_status "
995	                    "FROM contract_clauses c "
996	                    "JOIN contract_drafts d ON c.draft_id = d.draft_id "
997	                    "WHERE c.clause_id = ? AND d.org_id = ?",
998	                    (clause_id, org_id),
999	                ).fetchone()
1000	                if clause_row is None:
1001	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1002	                        f"clause {clause_id!r} is not accessible "
1003	                        f"to this caller"
1004	                    )
1005	                draft_status = ContractDraftStatus(clause_row["draft_status"])
1006	                if draft_status not in (
1007	                    ContractDraftStatus.AWAITING_APPROVAL,
1008	                ):
1009	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1010	                        f"clause decisions are only valid while the "
1011	                        f"draft is in AWAITING_APPROVAL; draft is "
1012	                        f"in {draft_status.value!r}"
1013	                    )
1014	                # Codex M-26 v12: trg_log_clause_decision_change
1015	                # writes the contract_decision_log row automatically
1016	                # whenever a clause's decision changes. We removed
1017	                # the manual INSERT here so the trigger is the
1018	                # single source of truth — direct-SQL bypasses also
1019	                # auto-log this way, closing the v11 audit-trail
1020	                # gap.
1021	                conn.execute(
1022	                    "UPDATE contract_clauses SET decision = ?, "
1023	                    "decided_by = ?, decision_notes = ?, "
1024	                    "decided_at = ? WHERE clause_id = ?",
1025	                    (
1026	                        decision.value, approver_user_id.strip(),
1027	                        sanitized_notes, now, clause_id,
1028	                    ),
1029	                )
1030	                conn.execute("COMMIT")
1031	            except Exception:
1032	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
1033	                raise
1034	        # Re-read the clause for the up-to-date row.
1035	        with self._connect() as conn:
1036	            row = conn.execute(
1037	                "SELECT * FROM contract_clauses WHERE clause_id = ?",
1038	                (clause_id,),
1039	            ).fetchone()
1040	        return _row_to_clause(row)

1047	        self, *, draft_id: str, org_id: str, submitter_user_id: str,
1048	    ) -> ContractDraft:

1057	        self,
1058	        *,
1059	        draft_id: str,
1060	        org_id: str,
1061	        approver_user_id: str,
1062	        rationale: str,
1063	    ) -> ContractDraft:
1064	        """Approve the whole draft. The FINAL_PLAN gate.
1065	
1066	        Refuses if (a) any clause is still PENDING, or (b) any
1067	        clause is REJECTED — every clause must be APPROVED.
1068	        Refuses if the approver is also the submitter (separation

1076	            )

1078	            draft_id=draft_id, org_id=org_id,
1079	            approver_user_id=approver_user_id, rationale=sanitized,
1080	        )

1101	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1102	    # Hardcoded transition helpers (Codex v6 structural refactor)
1103	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1104	    #
1105	    # Each `_perform_*` helper enforces ONE specific edge of the
1106	    # state machine. There is no parameter for "which transition"
1107	    # or "which bookkeeping flags" — the helper names ARE the
1108	    # transitions. A direct caller invoking `_perform_approve`
1109	    # cannot, by parameter manipulation, achieve any other edge
1110	    # because the helper only knows how to do its one job.
1111	    #
1112	    # All gate checks (state precondition, SOD, all-clauses-
1113	    # approved, rationale, clause-count) live INSIDE each helper's
1114	    # BEGIN IMMEDIATE so concurrent decide_clause / submit calls
1115	    # cannot create TOCTOU races.
1116	
1117	    def _perform_submit(
1118	        self, *, draft_id: str, org_id: str, submitter_user_id: str,
1119	    ) -> ContractDraft:
1120	        """Hardcoded edge: DRAFT → AWAITING_APPROVAL.
1121	
1122	        Preconditions enforced inside BEGIN IMMEDIATE:
1123	          - draft accessible to org_id
1124	          - draft.status == DRAFT
1125	          - draft has ≥ 1 clause
1126	
1127	        Invariants enforced (also at DB CHECK level):
1128	          - approved_by, rejected_by, decision_rationale,
1129	            decided_at remain NULL.
1130	          - status flips to AWAITING_APPROVAL.
1131	
1132	        This helper has NO parameter that could be used to set
1133	        decision metadata on a submitted draft. The v5 bypass
1134	        `_transition_draft(to_state=AWAITING_APPROVAL,
1135	        mark_decided=True, set_approver=True)` is structurally
1136	        impossible here — there is no `mark_decided` or
1137	        `set_approver` parameter.
1138	        """
1139	        if not submitter_user_id.strip():
1140	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
1141	                "submitter_user_id must be non-empty"
1142	            )
1143	        now = time.time()
1144	        with self._connect() as conn:
1145	            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
1146	            try:
1147	                row = conn.execute(
1148	                    "SELECT * FROM contract_drafts "
1149	                    "WHERE draft_id = ? AND org_id = ?",
1150	                    (draft_id, org_id),
1151	                ).fetchone()
1152	                if row is None:
1153	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1154	                        f"draft {draft_id!r} is not accessible "
1155	                        f"to this caller"
1156	                    )
1157	                current = ContractDraftStatus(row["status"])
1158	                if current != ContractDraftStatus.DRAFT:
1159	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1160	                        f"draft {draft_id!r} is in state "
1161	                        f"{current.value!r}; submit_for_approval "
1162	                        f"requires DRAFT state"
1163	                    )
1164	                clause_count = conn.execute(
1165	                    "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM contract_clauses "
1166	                    "WHERE draft_id = ?",
1167	                    (draft_id,),
1168	                ).fetchone()
1169	                if int(clause_count["n"]) == 0:
1170	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1171	                        f"draft {draft_id!r} has no clauses; "
1172	                        f"cannot submit an empty contract for "
1173	                        f"approval"
1174	                    )
1175	                # Codex M-26 v13: trg_log_draft_status_change
1176	                # auto-logs the draft→awaiting_approval transition.
1177	                # Manual INSERT removed to avoid duplicates.
1178	                conn.execute(
1179	                    "UPDATE contract_drafts SET status = ?, "
1180	                    "updated_at = ? WHERE draft_id = ?",
1181	                    (
1182	                        ContractDraftStatus.AWAITING_APPROVAL.value,
1183	                        now, draft_id,
1184	                    ),
1185	                )
1186	                conn.execute("COMMIT")
1187	            except Exception:
1188	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
1189	                raise
1190	        result = self.get_draft(draft_id=draft_id, org_id=org_id)
1191	        if result is None:
1192	            raise ContractDraftError(
1193	                f"submit succeeded but cannot read back draft "
1194	                f"{draft_id!r}"
1195	            )
1196	        return result
