1198	    def _perform_approve(
1199	        self,
1200	        *,
1201	        draft_id: str,
1202	        org_id: str,
1203	        approver_user_id: str,
1204	        rationale: str,
1205	    ) -> ContractDraft:
1206	        """Hardcoded edge: AWAITING_APPROVAL → APPROVED.
1207	
1208	        Preconditions enforced inside BEGIN IMMEDIATE:
1209	          - draft accessible to org_id
1210	          - draft.status == AWAITING_APPROVAL
1211	          - approver_user_id != draft.submitter_user_id (SOD)
1212	          - rationale content-non-empty (sanitized via
1213	            `_sanitize_notes`, which strips Cc/Cf and whitespace
1214	            so zero-width-space-only rationales are caught)
1215	          - all clauses APPROVED (no PENDING, no REJECTED)
1216	
1217	        Invariants written:
1218	          - status = 'approved'
1219	          - approved_by = approver_user_id
1220	          - decided_at = now
1221	          - decision_rationale = rationale (sanitized)
1222	          - rejected_by = NULL  (mutually exclusive with approved_by)
1223	
1224	        DB CHECK constraint + state-transition trigger on
1225	        contract_drafts mirror these invariants — even if this
1226	        Python helper had a bug, an attempted UPDATE that violates
1227	        the canonical APPROVED pattern OR the all-clauses-approved
1228	        invariant would fail at the SQL layer.
1229	        """
1230	        if not approver_user_id.strip():
1231	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
1232	                "approver_user_id must be non-empty"
1233	            )
1234	        # Codex v7 review fix: use _sanitize_notes (strips Unicode
1235	        # Cc/Cf categories + whitespace) instead of `.strip()`.
1236	        # `.strip()` only strips whitespace, so a rationale of
1237	        # "​" (zero-width space) was content-empty but passed
1238	        # `not rationale.strip()`. v7 routes direct-helper callers
1239	        # through the same sanitizer the public entry point uses.
1240	        sanitized_rationale = _sanitize_notes(rationale)
1241	        if sanitized_rationale is None:
1242	            raise ContractDraftStateError(
1243	                "approval rationale must be non-empty (LAW II — "
1244	                "every approval is part of the SOC2 audit trail)"
1245	            )
1246	        rationale = sanitized_rationale
1247	        now = time.time()
1248	        with self._connect() as conn:
1249	            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
1250	            try:
1251	                row = conn.execute(
1252	                    "SELECT * FROM contract_drafts "
1253	                    "WHERE draft_id = ? AND org_id = ?",
1254	                    (draft_id, org_id),
1255	                ).fetchone()
1256	                if row is None:
1257	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1258	                        f"draft {draft_id!r} is not accessible "
1259	                        f"to this caller"
1260	                    )
1261	                current = ContractDraftStatus(row["status"])
1262	                if current != ContractDraftStatus.AWAITING_APPROVAL:
1263	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1264	                        f"draft {draft_id!r} is in state "
1265	                        f"{current.value!r}; approve_draft requires "
1266	                        f"AWAITING_APPROVAL state"
1267	                    )
1268	                if row["submitter_user_id"] == approver_user_id.strip():
1269	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1270	                        "the contract submitter cannot approve "
1271	                        "their own draft (separation of duties — "
1272	                        "every approval needs a second human "
1273	                        "reviewer)"
1274	                    )
1275	                clause_rows = conn.execute(
1276	                    "SELECT decision FROM contract_clauses "
1277	                    "WHERE draft_id = ?",
1278	                    (draft_id,),
1279	                ).fetchall()
1280	                if not clause_rows:
1281	                    # Should be impossible — _perform_submit refuses
1282	                    # empty drafts. If we land here, something
1283	                    # corrupted the clause table after submit.
1284	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1285	                        "cannot approve draft: no clauses (this "
1286	                        "should be impossible — submit_for_approval "
1287	                        "refuses empty drafts)"
1288	                    )
1289	                decisions = {
1290	                    ClauseDecision(r["decision"]) for r in clause_rows
1291	                }
1292	                if ClauseDecision.PENDING in decisions:
1293	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1294	                        "cannot approve draft: at least one clause "
1295	                        "is still PENDING; decide every clause "
1296	                        "before approving"
1297	                    )
1298	                if ClauseDecision.REJECTED in decisions:
1299	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(
1300	                        "cannot approve draft: at least one clause "
1301	                        "is REJECTED; remove or replace it before "
1302	                        "approving"
1303	                    )
1304	                # Codex M-26 v13: trg_log_draft_status_change
1305	                # auto-logs the awaiting_approval→approved
1306	                # transition. Manual INSERT removed.
1307	                conn.execute(
1308	                    "UPDATE contract_drafts SET "
1309	                    "status = ?, updated_at = ?, decided_at = ?, "
1310	                    "decision_rationale = ?, "
1311	                    "approved_by = ?, rejected_by = NULL "
1312	                    "WHERE draft_id = ?",
1313	                    (
1314	                        ContractDraftStatus.APPROVED.value,
1315	                        now, now, rationale,
1316	                        approver_user_id.strip(), draft_id,
1317	                    ),
1318	                )
1319	                conn.execute("COMMIT")
1320	            except Exception:
1321	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
1322	                raise
1323	        result = self.get_draft(draft_id=draft_id, org_id=org_id)
1324	        if result is None:
1325	            raise ContractDraftError(
1326	                f"approve succeeded but cannot read back draft "
1327	                f"{draft_id!r}"
1328	            )
1329	        return result

1364	                "every rejection is part of the SOC2 audit trail)"
1365	            )
1366	        rationale = sanitized_rationale
1367	        now = time.time()
1368	        with self._connect() as conn:
1369	            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
1370	            try:
1371	                row = conn.execute(
1372	                    "SELECT * FROM contract_drafts "

1383	                    raise ContractDraftStateError(

1385	                        f"{current.value!r}; reject_draft requires "
1386	                        f"AWAITING_APPROVAL state"
1387	                    )
1388	                # Codex M-26 v13: trg_log_draft_status_change
1389	                # auto-logs the awaiting_approval→rejected
1390	                # transition. Manual INSERT removed.
1391	                conn.execute(
1392	                    "UPDATE contract_drafts SET "
1393	                    "status = ?, updated_at = ?, decided_at = ?, "
1394	                    "decision_rationale = ?, "
1395	                    "rejected_by = ?, approved_by = NULL "
1396	                    "WHERE draft_id = ?",
1397	                    (
1398	                        ContractDraftStatus.REJECTED.value,
1399	                        now, now, rationale,
1400	                        rejecter_user_id.strip(), draft_id,
1401	                    ),
1402	                )
1403	                conn.execute("COMMIT")
1404	            except Exception:
1405	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
1406	                raise
1407	        result = self.get_draft(draft_id=draft_id, org_id=org_id)
1408	        if result is None:

1419	    def assert_approved_for_send(
1420	        self, *, draft_id: str, org_id: str,
1421	    ) -> ContractDraft:
1422	        """The FINAL_PLAN's "mandatory human approval before
1423	        customer-facing" gate. Anywhere downstream code is about
1424	        to send / render / export a contract draft, it MUST call
1425	        this first. Raises ContractApprovalGateError if the draft
1426	        is not in APPROVED state.
1427	        """
1428	        draft = self.get_draft(draft_id=draft_id, org_id=org_id)
1429	        if draft is None:
1430	            raise ContractApprovalGateError(
1431	                f"draft {draft_id!r} is not accessible to this caller"
1432	            )
1433	        if draft.status != ContractDraftStatus.APPROVED:
1434	            raise ContractApprovalGateError(
1435	                f"draft {draft_id!r} is in state "
1436	                f"{draft.status.value!r}; only APPROVED drafts may "
1437	                f"be sent to a customer (FINAL_PLAN: mandatory human "
1438	                f"approval before customer-facing)"
1439	            )
1440	        return draft

1506	def _sanitize_notes(notes: str | None) -> str | None:
1507	    """Same notes-sanitization pattern as review_store: strip
1508	    Unicode Cc/Cf categories + whitespace; if nothing remains, the
1509	    input was content-empty."""
1510	    if notes is None:
1511	        return None
1512	    cleaned_chars = []
1513	    for ch in notes:
1514	        cat = unicodedata.category(ch)
1515	        if cat.startswith(("Cc", "Cf")):
1516	            continue
1517	        if ch.isspace():
1518	            continue
1519	        cleaned_chars.append(ch)
1520	    if not cleaned_chars:
1521	        return None
1522	    return notes.strip() or None
1523	
1524	
1525	def _row_to_draft(row: sqlite3.Row) -> ContractDraft:
1526	    return ContractDraft(
1527	        draft_id=row["draft_id"],
1528	        org_id=row["org_id"],
1529	        workspace_id=row["workspace_id"],

1531	        audit_run_id=row["audit_run_id"],
1532	        kind=ContractKind(row["kind"]),
1533	        title=row["title"],
1534	        counterparty_name=row["counterparty_name"],
1535	        status=ContractDraftStatus(row["status"]),
1536	        approved_by=row["approved_by"],
1537	        rejected_by=row["rejected_by"],
1538	        decision_rationale=row["decision_rationale"],
1539	        created_at=float(row["created_at"]),
1540	        updated_at=float(row["updated_at"]),
1541	        decided_at=(
1542	            float(row["decided_at"]) if row["decided_at"] is not None
1543	            else None
1544	        ),
1545	    )
1546	
1547	
1548	def _row_to_clause(row: sqlite3.Row) -> ContractClause:
1549	    evidence_ids = tuple(json.loads(row["evidence_ids_json"] or "[]"))
1550	    claim_ids = tuple(json.loads(row["claim_ids_json"] or "[]"))
1551	    return ContractClause(

1	"""Question-Bound Corpus Brief emitter (M-12 — Phase B).
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md:
4	  - "Narrow form: answer one user question over a selected corpus,
5	    emit cited brief"
6	  - "Per-paragraph inline citations OR explicit 'insufficient
7	    support' labels"
8	  - "NOT 'Workspace Brief' or 'WikiLLM' in product copy — those
9	    import wrong expectations"
10	
11	The brief is a sequence of paragraphs. Each paragraph is either:
12	  (a) a SUPPORTED claim with citations to retrieved chunks, OR
13	  (b) an INSUFFICIENT-SUPPORT label naming the sub-question that
14	      could not be answered.
15	
16	Why explicit-insufficient: per LAW II ("fail loud, no silent
17	fallbacks") the brief MUST NOT silently fabricate or hedge over a
18	gap. If retrieval returned nothing relevant for a sub-question,
19	the brief says so verbatim. Operators downstream see the gap and
20	can rephrase the question or upload more documents.
21	
22	Phase B implementation (deliberately simple):
23	  1. Retrieve top-K chunks (corpus_retriever.retrieve_chunks).
24	  2. If retrieval returned 0 chunks → emit a single
25	     INSUFFICIENT-SUPPORT paragraph saying so. No LLM call.
26	  3. Otherwise, ask the LLM to draft per-paragraph claims grounded
27	     in the retrieved chunks; structure: list of {claim, citations}.
28	  4. Validate every paragraph: each citation MUST reference a
29	     chunk_id from the retrieved set. Paragraphs whose claims
30	     cite an unknown chunk_id are dropped (per LAW II — never
31	     leak fabricated citations).
32	  5. If the validator drops every paragraph, emit a single
33	     INSUFFICIENT-SUPPORT paragraph instead of a hollow brief.
34	
35	Phase B does NOT call out to OpenRouter directly here; it accepts
36	a pluggable `LlmClient` protocol so tests can pass a fake. The
37	inspector_router wires the real OpenRouter client. This isolates
38	the brief logic from network dependencies and lets us exercise
39	the validation paths deterministically in tests.
40	"""
41	
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import json
45	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
46	from typing import Any, Protocol
47	
48	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.corpus_retriever import (
49	    DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE,
50	    DEFAULT_TOP_K,
51	    RetrievedChunk,
52	    retrieve_chunks,
53	)
54	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.workspace_store import WorkspaceStore
55	
56	
57	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
58	# Result records
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True)
63	class BriefCitation:
64	    """One citation inside a brief paragraph."""
65	
66	    chunk_id: str
67	    upload_id: str
68	    filename: str
69	    provenance: dict[str, Any]
70	
71	
72	@dataclass(frozen=True)
73	class BriefParagraph:
74	    """One paragraph in the brief.
75	
76	    Either:
77	      - claim is non-empty AND citations is non-empty (supported), OR
78	      - support_status == "insufficient_support" with a rationale
79	        in `claim` and no citations.
80	    """
81	
82	    claim: str
83	    citations: tuple[BriefCitation, ...] = field(default_factory=tuple)
84	    support_status: str = "supported"  # "supported" | "insufficient_support"
85	
86	
87	@dataclass(frozen=True)
88	class CorpusBrief:
89	    """Output of `compose_brief`."""
90	
91	    workspace_id: str
92	    question: str
93	    paragraphs: tuple[BriefParagraph, ...]
94	    retrieved_chunks: tuple[RetrievedChunk, ...]
95	
96	
97	def brief_to_dict(b: CorpusBrief) -> dict[str, Any]:
98	    """JSON-friendly serialization."""
99	    return {
100	        "workspace_id": b.workspace_id,
101	        "question": b.question,
102	        "paragraphs": [
103	            {
104	                "claim": p.claim,
105	                "support_status": p.support_status,
106	                "citations": [asdict(c) for c in p.citations],
107	            }
108	            for p in b.paragraphs
109	        ],
110	        "retrieved_chunks": [
111	            {
112	                "chunk_id": c.chunk_id,
113	                "upload_id": c.upload_id,
114	                "filename": c.filename,
115	                "score": c.score,
116	                "provenance": c.provenance,
117	                # Trim long text in the API response; full text is
118	                # available via /api/inspector/uploads/.../chunks.
119	                "text_preview": c.text[:300],
120	            }
121	            for c in b.retrieved_chunks
122	        ],
123	    }
124	
125	
126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	# LLM client protocol — pluggable for tests
128	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	
130	
131	class LlmClient(Protocol):
132	    """Minimal LLM interface the brief emitter needs.
133	
134	    Codex M-12 review fix: `draft_brief` is now async because the
135	    real OpenRouter client is async. Sync test fakes implement
136	    this as `async def` too — there's no overhead since they don't
137	    actually do I/O.
138	
139	    Implementations must:
140	      - Return JSON parseable as the schema described in the prompt.
141	      - Raise on any error (no silent fallbacks per LAW II).
142	    """
143	
144	    async def draft_brief(
145	        self,
146	        question: str,
147	        chunks: list[RetrievedChunk],
148	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
149	        """Return a list of paragraph dicts:
150	          [{"claim": str, "citations": [{"chunk_id": str}, ...]}, ...]
151	        Raises on failure (timeout, malformed response, etc.).
152	        """
153	        ...
154	
155	
156	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
157	# Brief composition
158	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
159	
160	
161	_INSUFFICIENT_NO_RETRIEVAL = (
162	    "Insufficient support in this workspace for the question. No "
163	    "uploaded documents contained content relevant to the query. "
164	    "Upload more documents or rephrase the question."
165	)
166	
167	_INSUFFICIENT_LLM_DROPPED_ALL = (
168	    "Insufficient support: the corpus contains chunks lexically "
169	    "related to the question, but none of the drafted claims could "
170	    "be tied back to a retrieved chunk. Operator should rephrase "
171	    "the question or review the corpus directly."
172	)
173	
174	
175	async def compose_brief(
176	    store: WorkspaceStore,
177	    workspace_id: str,
178	    question: str,
179	    llm: LlmClient,
180	    top_k: int = DEFAULT_TOP_K,
181	    min_score: float = DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE,
182	) -> CorpusBrief:
183	    """Compose a Question-Bound Corpus Brief over the workspace.
184	
185	    Returns a CorpusBrief whose paragraphs are either supported
186	    (with non-empty citations) or labeled "insufficient_support".
187	    Never silently fabricates citations.
188	
189	    Codex M-12 review fix: now async so it can `await` the real
190	    OpenRouter LLM client. Test fakes implement `draft_brief` as
191	    async too.
192	
193	    Raises ValueError on unknown workspace (matches retrieve_chunks
194	    behavior so the API layer maps to 404).
195	    """
196	    chunks = retrieve_chunks(
197	        store=store, workspace_id=workspace_id, question=question,
198	        top_k=top_k, min_score=min_score,
199	    )
200	
201	    if not chunks:
202	        return CorpusBrief(
203	            workspace_id=workspace_id, question=question,
204	            paragraphs=(BriefParagraph(
205	                claim=_INSUFFICIENT_NO_RETRIEVAL,
206	                support_status="insufficient_support",
207	            ),),
208	            retrieved_chunks=(),
209	        )
210	
211	    raw_paragraphs = await llm.draft_brief(question=question, chunks=chunks)
212	
213	    # Codex M-12 v2 review fix: an upload can be soft-deleted DURING
214	    # the LLM await. Re-snapshot the eligible-chunk set AFTER the
215	    # await and intersect — any chunk that was deleted while the
216	    # LLM was running is removed from the validation set so the
217	    # final brief never cites a chunk that's no longer eligible.
218	    eligible_now = store.list_eligible_chunks(workspace_id)
219	    eligible_ids_now = {c["chunk_id"] for c in eligible_now}
220	    chunks_by_id = {
221	        c.chunk_id: c for c in chunks if c.chunk_id in eligible_ids_now
222	    }
223	
224	    validated: list[BriefParagraph] = []
225	    for p in raw_paragraphs:
226	        if not isinstance(p, dict):
227	            continue
228	        claim = (p.get("claim") or "").strip()
229	        if not claim:
230	            continue
231	        cites_raw = p.get("citations") or []
232	        if not isinstance(cites_raw, list):
233	            continue
234	        valid_cites: list[BriefCitation] = []
235	        seen_ids: set[str] = set()
236	        for c in cites_raw:
237	            if not isinstance(c, dict):
238	                continue
239	            cid = c.get("chunk_id")
240	            if not isinstance(cid, str) or cid in seen_ids:
241	                continue
242	            chunk = chunks_by_id.get(cid)
243	            if chunk is None:
244	                # Codex M-12 review fix: never leak fabricated
245	                # chunk_ids. Drop the citation; the paragraph as a
246	                # whole still requires ≥1 valid citation to count.
247	                continue
248	            seen_ids.add(cid)
249	            valid_cites.append(BriefCitation(
250	                chunk_id=chunk.chunk_id,
251	                upload_id=chunk.upload_id,
252	                filename=chunk.filename,
253	                provenance=chunk.provenance,
254	            ))
255	        if not valid_cites:
256	            continue
257	        validated.append(BriefParagraph(
258	            claim=claim,
259	            citations=tuple(valid_cites),
260	            support_status="supported",
261	        ))
262	
263	    # Codex M-12 v2 review fix: filter retrieved_chunks to the
264	    # post-await eligible set so the API response never advertises
265	    # chunks that are no longer eligible for retrieval (e.g. their
266	    # upload was soft-deleted during the LLM await).
267	    surviving_retrieved = tuple(
268	        c for c in chunks if c.chunk_id in eligible_ids_now
269	    )
270	
271	    if not validated:
272	        # LLM hallucinated all citations OR returned no usable
273	        # paragraphs (or all of them got pruned by the post-await
274	        # delete check). Emit explicit insufficient-support label
275	        # rather than a hollow brief.
276	        return CorpusBrief(
277	            workspace_id=workspace_id, question=question,
278	            paragraphs=(BriefParagraph(
279	                claim=_INSUFFICIENT_LLM_DROPPED_ALL,
280	                support_status="insufficient_support",
281	            ),),
282	            retrieved_chunks=surviving_retrieved,
283	        )
284	
285	    return CorpusBrief(
286	        workspace_id=workspace_id, question=question,
287	        paragraphs=tuple(validated),
288	        retrieved_chunks=surviving_retrieved,
289	    )
290	
291	
292	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
293	# Default OpenRouter-backed LLM client (used by the API)
294	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
295	
296	
297	_DRAFT_BRIEF_SYSTEM = (
298	    "You are a research assistant drafting a tightly-cited brief. "
299	    "Every claim you write MUST cite at least one chunk by its "
300	    "chunk_id. Never invent chunk_ids; only cite chunks the user "
301	    "provided. If a sub-question cannot be answered from the chunks, "
302	    "OMIT that paragraph rather than hedging or fabricating. "
303	    "Output JSON of the exact form: "
304	    '{"paragraphs": [{"claim": "...", "citations": [{"chunk_id": "..."}]}]}.'
305	)
306	
307	
308	def _build_user_prompt(question: str, chunks: list[RetrievedChunk]) -> str:
309	    """Build a deterministic user prompt for the LLM."""
310	    lines = [
311	        f"Question: {question}",
312	        "",
313	        "Available chunks (cite by chunk_id):",
314	    ]
315	    for c in chunks:
316	        lines.append(
317	            f"---\nchunk_id: {c.chunk_id}\nfilename: {c.filename}\n"
318	            f"text:\n{c.text}\n"
319	        )
320	    lines.append("---")
321	    lines.append(
322	        "Respond with JSON only. Each paragraph cites at least one "
323	        "chunk_id from the list above."
324	    )
325	    return "\n".join(lines)
326	
327	
328	class OpenRouterBriefClient:
329	    """Real LLM client backed by OpenRouterClient.generate.
330	
331	    Codex M-12 review fix: now async, awaits `generate()`, and
332	    parses `LLMResponse.content`. v1 incorrectly:
333	      - Called `asyncio.run()` from inside an async FastAPI route
334	        (raises "asyncio.run() cannot be called from a running
335	        event loop").
336	      - Treated the `LLMResponse` return value as a str
337	        (`AttributeError` on `.strip()`).
338	
339	    Constructed lazily by the API layer so unit tests don't need
340	    network credentials.
341	
342	    Per LAW II: any LLM error (timeout, malformed JSON, schema
343	    mismatch) propagates — we never fall back to a stub paragraph.
344	    """
345	
346	    def __init__(self, openrouter_client: Any) -> None:
347	        """`openrouter_client` is an instance of
348	        `src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client.OpenRouterClient`.
349	        Typed loosely to avoid pulling that import here."""
350	        self._client = openrouter_client
351	
352	    async def draft_brief(
353	        self, question: str, chunks: list[RetrievedChunk]
354	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
355	        prompt = _build_user_prompt(question, chunks)
356	        # Inline call via the existing async client. We use generate
357	        # (not generate_structured) and parse the JSON ourselves so
358	        # this module doesn't import a Pydantic schema from the
359	        # OpenRouter client family — keeps the dependency arrows
360	        # clean.
361	        # Codex M-12 v2 review fix: removed `thinking_mode=False`
362	        # — the real OpenRouterClient.generate signature has no
363	        # such kwarg (would TypeError at call time and 500 the
364	        # endpoint). generate() already disables reasoning by
365	        # default for the prose path.
366	        response = await self._client.generate(
367	            prompt=prompt, system=_DRAFT_BRIEF_SYSTEM,
368	            max_tokens=4096,
369	        )
370	        # OpenRouterClient.generate returns LLMResponse, not str.
371	        text = getattr(response, "content", None)
372	        if text is None:
373	            # Tolerate plain-string returns from test doubles that
374	            # haven't migrated to LLMResponse-shaped fakes.
375	            text = response if isinstance(response, str) else ""
376	        if not isinstance(text, str):
377	            raise ValueError(
378	                f"LLM response.content must be str; got {type(text).__name__}"
379	            )
380	        cleaned = text.strip()
381	        if cleaned.startswith("```"):
382	            cleaned = cleaned.strip("`")
383	            cleaned = cleaned.split("\n", 1)[1] if "\n" in cleaned else cleaned
384	            # Trim trailing fence remnants.
385	            cleaned = cleaned.rstrip("`").strip()
386	        parsed = json.loads(cleaned)
387	        if not isinstance(parsed, dict) or "paragraphs" not in parsed:
388	            raise ValueError(
389	                "LLM brief response missing 'paragraphs' key; refusing "
390	                "to silently fabricate"
391	            )
392	        paragraphs = parsed["paragraphs"]
393	        if not isinstance(paragraphs, list):
394	            raise ValueError("LLM 'paragraphs' field must be a list")
395	        return paragraphs
396

1	"""Corpus retriever (M-12 — Phase B).
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md: Question-Bound Corpus Brief = "answer one user
4	question over a SELECTED corpus, emit cited brief". This module
5	handles the retrieval half: given a question + workspace_id, return
6	the top-K chunks ranked by relevance, each carrying its
7	(upload_id, provenance) so M-12's brief emitter can cite back to
8	exact locations.
9	
10	Phase B uses lexical BM25-style retrieval (no embeddings — keeps
11	the dependency surface small and the failure mode obvious).
12	Phase C M-12.5 will swap in a vector backend; the public API
13	(`retrieve_chunks`) is stable so callers don't change.
14	
15	Why BM25, not embeddings, for Phase B:
16	  - Zero new model deps; deterministic results in tests.
17	  - For Phase B's narrow scope (10-50 docs/workspace, single
18	    question per call), recall on lexical matches is acceptable.
19	  - When M-12 emits "insufficient support" because BM25 missed a
20	    semantically-paraphrased chunk, that's a Phase B feature, not
21	    a bug — the operator sees the gap and can rephrase.
22	
23	Workflow boundary:
24	  - retrieve_chunks() ONLY reads; never mutates the workspace.
25	  - It does NOT decide "answered" vs "insufficient" — that's the
26	    verifier's job (corpus_brief.py).
27	  - It returns chunks from non-deleted, parsed uploads only.
28	
29	The tokenizer is intentionally identical to template_classifier's
30	(hyphen split, Unicode hyphen normalize, stopword filter, Roman
31	+ compact-drug-class normalization) so retrieval ranks consistent
32	with the routing classifier's notion of content tokens.
33	"""
34	
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import math
38	from collections import Counter
39	from dataclasses import dataclass
40	from typing import Any
41	
42	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.template_classifier import (
43	    _filter_stopwords,
44	    _tokenize_raw,
45	)
46	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.workspace_store import WorkspaceStore
47	
48	
49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	# Result types
51	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	
53	
54	@dataclass(frozen=True)
55	class RetrievedChunk:
56	    """One scored chunk pulled from the workspace.
57	
58	    Carries everything the brief emitter needs to cite:
59	      - chunk_id / upload_id: stable IDs for downstream use.
60	      - filename: human-readable label for the citation.
61	      - text: the chunk content (used by the verifier prompt).
62	      - provenance: location info (TextSpan, PdfSpan, ...).
63	      - score: BM25 score; UI may show this for debugging.
64	    """
65	
66	    chunk_id: str
67	    upload_id: str
68	    filename: str
69	    text: str
70	    provenance: dict[str, Any]
71	    score: float
72	
73	
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	# BM25 ranker
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	
78	
79	# Standard Robertson-Sparck-Jones BM25 parameters.
80	_BM25_K1 = 1.5
81	_BM25_B = 0.75
82	
83	
84	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
85	    """Tokenize text the same way the template classifier does, then
86	    drop stopwords and 1-char tokens. Returns a list (order
87	    preserved) so we can compute term frequencies; the query side
88	    uses a frozenset for the same vocabulary."""
89	    toks_set = _tokenize_raw(text)
90	    # _tokenize_raw is set-based; we need a multiset for BM25.
91	    # Re-tokenize directly from the same primitive.
92	    from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.template_classifier import _tokenize_raw_seq
93	    seq = _tokenize_raw_seq(text)
94	    return [t for t in seq if t in _filter_stopwords(toks_set)]
95	
96	
97	def _bm25_score(
98	    query_tokens: list[str],
99	    chunk_tokens: list[str],
100	    doc_freqs: dict[str, int],
101	    n_docs: int,
102	    avg_doc_len: float,
103	) -> float:
104	    """Standard BM25 score for one query against one chunk."""
105	    if not chunk_tokens or not query_tokens or n_docs == 0:
106	        return 0.0
107	    chunk_counter = Counter(chunk_tokens)
108	    chunk_len = len(chunk_tokens)
109	    score = 0.0
110	    seen_terms: set[str] = set()
111	    for term in query_tokens:
112	        if term in seen_terms:
113	            continue
114	        seen_terms.add(term)
115	        df = doc_freqs.get(term, 0)
116	        if df == 0:
117	            continue
118	        # Robertson-Sparck-Jones IDF; adds 1 to keep results
119	        # non-negative even for very common terms.
120	        idf = math.log((n_docs - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1.0)
121	        tf = chunk_counter.get(term, 0)
122	        if tf == 0:
123	            continue
124	        denom = tf + _BM25_K1 * (
125	            1 - _BM25_B + _BM25_B * (chunk_len / avg_doc_len)
126	        )
127	        score += idf * (tf * (_BM25_K1 + 1) / denom)
128	    return score
129	
130	
131	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# Public API
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	
136	DEFAULT_TOP_K = 8
137	DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE = 0.5  # BM25 floor for inclusion
138	
139	
140	def retrieve_chunks(
141	    store: WorkspaceStore,
142	    workspace_id: str,
143	    question: str,
144	    top_k: int = DEFAULT_TOP_K,
145	    min_score: float = DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE,
146	) -> list[RetrievedChunk]:
147	    """Return up to top_k highest-scoring chunks from the workspace
148	    that pass `min_score`. Empty list if nothing crosses the floor.
149	
150	    Drops chunks from soft-deleted or non-`parsed` uploads.
151	
152	    The workspace MUST exist; raises ValueError otherwise so the
153	    API layer can return a clean 404. (Per LAW II — never silently
154	    return empty results when the workspace is missing.)
155	    """
156	    if store.get_workspace(workspace_id) is None:
157	        raise ValueError(f"unknown workspace: {workspace_id}")
158	
159	    q_tokens = _content_tokens(question)
160	    if not q_tokens:
161	        return []
162	
163	    # Codex M-12 review fix: atomic single-query snapshot of all
164	    # eligible chunks (parsed + non-deleted uploads in this
165	    # workspace). Replaces the v1 two-phase pattern which raced
166	    # soft-delete: a delete landing between list_uploads() and
167	    # list_chunks() let the deleted upload's chunks leak.
168	    eligible = store.list_eligible_chunks(workspace_id)
169	    if not eligible:
170	        return []
171	
172	    all_chunks: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], list[str], str]] = []
173	    # tuple shape: (chunk_record, tokens, filename)
174	    for ck in eligible:
175	        tokens = _content_tokens(ck["text"])
176	        all_chunks.append((ck, tokens, ck["filename"]))
177	
178	    if not all_chunks:
179	        return []
180	
181	    n_docs = len(all_chunks)
182	    avg_doc_len = sum(len(t) for _, t, _ in all_chunks) / n_docs
183	    if avg_doc_len == 0:
184	        return []
185	
186	    # Doc-frequency table — count ONCE per chunk.
187	    doc_freqs: dict[str, int] = {}
188	    for _, tokens, _ in all_chunks:
189	        for term in set(tokens):
190	            doc_freqs[term] = doc_freqs.get(term, 0) + 1
191	
192	    scored: list[RetrievedChunk] = []
193	    for ck, tokens, filename in all_chunks:
194	        s = _bm25_score(q_tokens, tokens, doc_freqs, n_docs, avg_doc_len)
195	        if s < min_score:
196	            continue
197	        scored.append(RetrievedChunk(
198	            chunk_id=ck["chunk_id"],
199	            upload_id=ck["upload_id"],
200	            filename=filename,
201	            text=ck["text"],
202	            provenance=ck["provenance"],
203	            score=s,
204	        ))
205	    scored.sort(key=lambda c: c.score, reverse=True)
206	    return scored[:top_k]
207

2	
3	M-D3 phase 1 (`decision_telemetry.py`, commit 212102d) shipped
4	the per-workspace SQLite DecisionRecordStore. Records-level
5	APIs (record_decision, update_curator_action, get,
6	list_for_workspace, count_for_workspace) are all in place.
7

9	acceptance/modification/override/rejection rates, optionally
10	filtered by DecisionKind and time-windowed. Pure substrate
11	on top of the M-D3 phase 1 store.
12	
13	## Why this milestone matters
14	
15	M-D4 (auto-trust gate) is calendar-blocked on accumulating ≥6
16	months of telemetry. When M-D4 ships, it will need aggregate
17	metrics like "what fraction of induction decisions in this
18	workspace over the last 90 days were accepted_as_proposed?"
19	to decide whether the system is trustworthy enough to bypass
20	the operator-review queue for that workspace.
21	
22	Phase 2 v1 ships the substrate query API M-D4 will consume.
23	No M-D4 calibration logic here — just the deterministic
24	aggregation primitive.
25	
26	## What v1 ships
27	
28	  - `DecisionAggregates` dataclass — counts + rates per
29	    terminal action, plus pending count, plus
30	    total_decisions and total_terminal aggregates
31	  - `compute_aggregates(store, workspace_id, *,
32	    decision_kind, since, until)` — pure derivation backed
33	    by the M-D3 phase 1 store query API
34	
35	## Substrate boundary
36	
37	Imports `decision_telemetry` (CuratorAction, DecisionKind,
38	DecisionRecordStore, DecisionTelemetryError) only. No new DB
39	schema; reuses the phase 1 store's queries. No LLM, no HTTP.
40	
41	See `docs/md3_phase2_threat_model.md` for boundaries.
42	"""
43	
44	from __future__ import annotations
45	
46	import time
47	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
48	
49	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.decision_telemetry import (
50	    CuratorAction,
51	    DecisionKind,

54	    DecisionTelemetryError,

58	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
59	# Errors
60	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	
62	
63	class DecisionAggregatesError(ValueError):
64	    """Raised on contract violations — invalid window, etc."""
65	
66	
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	# Aggregates dataclass
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	
71	
72	@dataclass(frozen=True)
73	class DecisionAggregates:
74	    """Per-window decision aggregates.
75	
76	    `total_decisions` is total records in the window (including
77	    PENDING — i.e. records the curator hasn't yet acted on).
78	    `total_terminal` is the subset that has reached a terminal
79	    `CuratorAction`. The 4 terminal counts sum to `total_terminal`.
80	    `pending_count` is `total_decisions - total_terminal`.
81	
82	    `acceptance_rate`, `modification_rate`, `override_rate`,
83	    `rejection_rate` are each `count / total_terminal`. They
84	    are None when `total_terminal == 0` (rates are undefined
85	    on an empty terminal set — surfacing 0.0 would be
86	    misleading: "0% accepted because we haven't decided
87	    anything" is different from "0% accepted because curator
88	    rejects everything").
89	
90	    `window_start` / `window_end` are the time bounds applied
91	    to the query (UNIX epoch floats, same convention as
92	    DecisionRecord.created_at). `None` means open on that
93	    end.
94	
95	    `decision_kind` is None for "all kinds aggregated" or a
96	    specific kind for filtered aggregates.
97	    """
98	
99	    workspace_id: str
100	    decision_kind: DecisionKind | None
101	    window_start: float | None
102	    window_end: float | None
103	    total_decisions: int
104	    total_terminal: int
105	    pending_count: int
106	    accepted_count: int
107	    modified_count: int
108	    overridden_count: int
109	    rejected_count: int
110	    acceptance_rate: float | None
111	    modification_rate: float | None
112	    override_rate: float | None
113	    rejection_rate: float | None
114	
115	
116	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
117	# Public API
118	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
119	
120	
121	def compute_aggregates(
122	    store: DecisionRecordStore,
123	    workspace_id: str,
124	    *,
125	    decision_kind: DecisionKind | None = None,
126	    since: float | None = None,
127	    until: float | None = None,
128	) -> DecisionAggregates:
129	    """Compute decision aggregates for `workspace_id`.
130	
131	    `decision_kind` filters to one kind (induction vs
132	    scope_gate); None aggregates across both.
133	
134	    `since` and `until` are UNIX epoch floats (matching
135	    `DecisionRecord.created_at`). Records with
136	    `created_at < since` or `created_at > until` are excluded.
137	    Both bounds are inclusive on the boundary. Either can be
138	    None for an open window. `since == until` selects records
139	    captured exactly at that instant (rare but valid).
140	
141	    Pure derivation backed by the M-D3 phase 1 store's
142	    `list_for_workspace` API. No new SQL paths added — the
143	    aggregation is computed in Python over the returned
144	    record list. This trades query efficiency for code
145	    simplicity at the v1 stage; v2 may add a SQL-side
146	    aggregation query if record counts grow.
147	    """
148	    if not isinstance(store, DecisionRecordStore):
149	        raise DecisionAggregatesError(
150	            f"store must be DecisionRecordStore, got "
151	            f"{type(store).__name__}"
152	        )
153	    # Codex round-2 LOW fix (v3): align validation with phase 1
154	    # store. Phase 1's record_decision uses `if not workspace_id`
155	    # (rejects "" but accepts "   "). v2 used `not workspace_id
156	    # or not workspace_id.strip()`, rejecting whitespace-only IDs
157	    # — which means phase 2 couldn't aggregate phase-1-valid rows
158	    # written under whitespace-only IDs. v3 matches phase 1
159	    # semantics exactly.
160	    if not workspace_id:
161	        raise DecisionAggregatesError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
162	    if since is not None and until is not None and since > until:
163	        raise DecisionAggregatesError(
164	            f"since ({since}) must be <= until ({until})"
165	        )
166	
167	    # Codex round-1 MEDIUM fix (v2): pass workspace_id THROUGH
168	    # to the phase 1 store verbatim. v1 stripped before query,
169	    # but phase 1's `record_decision` persists workspace IDs
170	    # verbatim — so rows written under padded IDs like
171	    # `"  ws1  "` were invisible to aggregation that
172	    # compared against the stripped `"ws1"`. We now match
173	    # phase 1 store semantics: don't normalize, don't strip.
174	    # The dataclass echoes the input verbatim too.
175	    ws = workspace_id
176	
177	    # Use the v1 store's query API. Pull all records (no limit)
178	    # for the workspace+kind filter, then filter+aggregate in
179	    # Python.
180	    records = store.list_for_workspace(
181	        ws, decision_kind=decision_kind,
182	    )
183	
184	    counts = {
185	        CuratorAction.PENDING: 0,
186	        CuratorAction.ACCEPTED_AS_PROPOSED: 0,
187	        CuratorAction.MODIFIED: 0,
188	        CuratorAction.OVERRIDDEN: 0,
189	        CuratorAction.REJECTED: 0,
190	    }
191	    total_decisions = 0
192	
193	    for rec in records:
194	        if since is not None and rec.created_at < since:
195	            continue
196	        if until is not None and rec.created_at > until:
197	            continue
198	        total_decisions += 1
199	        counts[rec.curator_action] = counts[rec.curator_action] + 1
200	
201	    pending = counts[CuratorAction.PENDING]
202	    accepted = counts[CuratorAction.ACCEPTED_AS_PROPOSED]
203	    modified = counts[CuratorAction.MODIFIED]
204	    overridden = counts[CuratorAction.OVERRIDDEN]
205	    rejected = counts[CuratorAction.REJECTED]
206	    total_terminal = accepted + modified + overridden + rejected
207	
208	    if total_terminal == 0:
209	        acceptance_rate = None
210	        modification_rate = None
211	        override_rate = None
212	        rejection_rate = None
213	    else:
214	        acceptance_rate = accepted / total_terminal
215	        modification_rate = modified / total_terminal
216	        override_rate = overridden / total_terminal
217	        rejection_rate = rejected / total_terminal
218	
219	    return DecisionAggregates(
220	        workspace_id=ws,
221	        decision_kind=decision_kind,
222	        window_start=since,
223	        window_end=until,
224	        total_decisions=total_decisions,
225	        total_terminal=total_terminal,
226	        pending_count=pending,
227	        accepted_count=accepted,
228	        modified_count=modified,
229	        overridden_count=overridden,
230	        rejected_count=rejected,
231	        acceptance_rate=acceptance_rate,
232	        modification_rate=modification_rate,
233	        override_rate=override_rate,
234	        rejection_rate=rejection_rate,
235	    )
236

1	"""M-D3 (Phase D): Induction → operator-review telemetry — bootstrap.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN M-D3 + Phase D milestones plan: record every
4	contract induction and (per advisor watch-out) every scope-gate
5	decision, paired with the curator's action when it ships through
6	M-23 review queue. The data substrate is the **calibration input
7	for M-D4** (auto-trust gate), which is calendar-blocked on ≥6
8	months of M-D3 telemetry.
9	
10	Phase 1 ships **telemetry substrate only**. Trust-gate logic is
11	deferred to M-D4. Recorded confidences are uncalibrated by
12	design — calibration is precisely what M-D4 will use this data
13	for. Anyone using M-D3 telemetry to gate live audits before
14	M-D4 ships is misusing the substrate.
15	
16	## Why a generic DecisionKind discriminator
17	
18	Per advisor: `M-D3 sits under M-D2 inductor, but M-D5 also sits
19	under M-D20 router with classifier above. Two ways to handle:
20	(a) generic enough record shape from v1 (preferred — decision_kind
21	discriminator), or (b) document explicitly that M-D3 phase 1
22	records only induction decisions and scope-gate decisions are
23	M-D3 phase 2.`
24	
25	Option (a) — `decision_kind: DecisionKind = induction | scope_gate`
26	adds one column and avoids a forced phase 2 just to add scope-
27	gate fields when M-D5 phase 2 ships.
28	
29	## Coupling to M-21 / M-23
30	
31	Same SQLite per-workspace substrate as M-21 / M-D7 / M-D10.
32	One extra `decision_records` table. The M-23 review queue closes
33	contract reviews; when that happens, callers update the matching
34	`DecisionRecord.curator_action` via `update_curator_action()`.
35	M-D3 does NOT re-implement the queue; it records what happens
36	*after* the queue closes.
37	
38	## What's in the record
39	
40	For both induction and scope_gate kinds:
41	  - `proposed_payload`: what the system suggested (contract dict
42	    for induction; GatedMatchResult action+template_id+rationale
43	    for scope_gate)
44	  - `proposed_confidence`: classifier/inductor self-reported
45	    confidence in [0, 1]
46	  - `curator_action`: accepted_as_proposed / modified / overridden
47	    / rejected / pending
48	  - `final_payload`: what shipped (or None if pending/rejected)
49	  - `diff_payload`: structural diff between proposed + final (None
50	    when curator_action == accepted_as_proposed)
51	  - `actor_user_id`: who made the curator decision (None for
52	    pending; required for non-pending actions)
53	  - `notes`: curator's free-text rationale
54	
55	## What's NOT in phase 1
56	
57	- Trust-gate logic ("if this template_class has ≥95% acceptance
58	  rate over 6mo, auto-confirm"). That's M-D4.
59	- Live calibration of inductor/classifier confidence. Phase 1
60	  records uncalibrated values; calibration is M-D4's job.
61	- Cross-workspace aggregation. Each workspace has its own DB
62	  file; aggregation queries are deferred to M-D4 / M-D13.
63	- Real-time alerts on drift. M-D10 covers freshness alerts;
64	  decision-drift alerts are M-D4 territory.
65	- Notification callbacks (email curator when their review queue
66	  has waiting items). M-23 ships its own notifications; M-D3
67	  is record-only.
68	
69	See `docs/md3_phase1_threat_model.md` for the full boundary set.
70	"""
71	
72	from __future__ import annotations
73	
74	import json
75	import sqlite3
76	import time
77	import uuid
78	from dataclasses import dataclass
79	from enum import Enum
80	from pathlib import Path
81	from typing import Any
82	
83	
84	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
85	# Errors
86	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
87	
88	
89	class DecisionTelemetryError(Exception):
90	    """Raised on schema/state violations."""
91	
92	
93	class DecisionTelemetryStateError(DecisionTelemetryError):
94	    """Raised when an invalid state transition is attempted (e.g. updating
95	    a non-existent record, or moving from a terminal action)."""
96	
97	
98	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99	# Enumerations
100	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101	
102	
103	class DecisionKind(str, Enum):
104	    """Closed taxonomy of decision sources.
105	
106	    Generic discriminator (advisor watch-out): `M-D3 records
107	    induction decisions today; M-D5's gate decisions land here too
108	    once phase 2 of M-D5 ships a concrete classifier. Generic
109	    record shape avoids a forced phase 2 to add scope-gate columns.`
110	    """
111	
112	    INDUCTION = "induction"
113	    SCOPE_GATE = "scope_gate"
114	
115	
116	class CuratorAction(str, Enum):
117	    """What a human curator did with the system's proposal.
118	
119	    `pending` is the initial state on `record_decision`. Once a
120	    curator transitions through M-23, callers update via
121	    `update_curator_action()` to one of the four terminal states.
122	
123	    Terminal states (no further transitions allowed):
124	      - `accepted_as_proposed`: curator shipped exactly what the
125	        system suggested. No diff.
126	      - `modified`: curator shipped a modified version. `diff_payload`
127	        captures the change.
128	      - `overridden`: curator chose a different decision entirely
129	        (e.g. system suggested ROUTE, curator forced REJECT via
130	        the operator-override path).
131	      - `rejected`: curator rejected the run entirely; nothing
132	        shipped to the user.
133	    """
134	
135	    PENDING = "pending"
136	    ACCEPTED_AS_PROPOSED = "accepted_as_proposed"
137	    MODIFIED = "modified"
138	    OVERRIDDEN = "overridden"
139	    REJECTED = "rejected"
140	
141	
142	_TERMINAL_ACTIONS: frozenset[CuratorAction] = frozenset({
143	    CuratorAction.ACCEPTED_AS_PROPOSED,
144	    CuratorAction.MODIFIED,
145	    CuratorAction.OVERRIDDEN,
146	    CuratorAction.REJECTED,
147	})
148	
149	
150	def _validate_terminal_args(
151	    curator_action: CuratorAction,
152	    actor_user_id: str,
153	    final_payload: dict[str, Any] | None,
154	    diff_payload: dict[str, Any] | None,
155	) -> None:
156	    """Codex round-1 MED fix (v2): centralized cross-action invariant
157	    enforcement, called by update_curator_action() before any DB
158	    mutation. Every terminal-action invariant lives here so the
159	    contract is auditable in one place.
160	
161	    Invariants enforced:
162	      - curator_action MUST be a CuratorAction enum value (not a string)
163	      - curator_action MUST NOT be PENDING (this is a transition to
164	        terminal, not a re-entry)
165	      - actor_user_id MUST be non-empty for any terminal action
166	      - REJECTED: final_payload + diff_payload MUST both be None
167	      - ACCEPTED_AS_PROPOSED: final_payload required (= proposed),
168	        diff_payload MUST be None
169	      - MODIFIED / OVERRIDDEN: final_payload required, diff_payload
170	        is optional but expected
171	    """
172	    if not isinstance(curator_action, CuratorAction):
173	        raise DecisionTelemetryError(
174	            f"curator_action must be CuratorAction, got "
175	            f"{type(curator_action).__name__}"
176	        )
177	    if curator_action == CuratorAction.PENDING:
178	        raise DecisionTelemetryError(
179	            "update_curator_action cannot transition to PENDING"
180	        )
181	    if not actor_user_id:
182	        raise DecisionTelemetryError(
183	            "actor_user_id required for terminal curator actions"
184	        )
185	    if curator_action == CuratorAction.REJECTED:
186	        if final_payload is not None:
187	            raise DecisionTelemetryError(
188	                "final_payload must be None for rejected action"
189	            )
190	        if diff_payload is not None:
191	            raise DecisionTelemetryError(
192	                "diff_payload must be None for rejected action"
193	            )
194	        return
195	    if curator_action == CuratorAction.ACCEPTED_AS_PROPOSED:
196	        if diff_payload is not None:
197	            raise DecisionTelemetryError(
198	                "diff_payload must be None for accepted_as_proposed "
199	                "(no diff exists when curator accepts as-is)"
200	            )
201	        if final_payload is None:
202	            raise DecisionTelemetryError(
203	                "final_payload required for accepted_as_proposed; "
204	                "should equal proposed_payload"
205	            )
206	        return
207	    # MODIFIED or OVERRIDDEN
208	    if final_payload is None:
209	        raise DecisionTelemetryError(
210	            f"final_payload required for {curator_action.value}"
211	        )

225	      - induction kind: induced contract dict
226	      - scope_gate kind: GatedMatchResult-shaped dict

229	    `proposed_confidence` is in [0, 1] — uncalibrated. See
230	    threat-model boundary 2.

286	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS decision_records (
287	    record_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
288	    workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL,
289	    decision_kind TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (decision_kind IN ({_VALID_KINDS})),
290	    query TEXT NOT NULL,
291	    proposed_payload_json TEXT NOT NULL,
292	    proposed_confidence REAL NOT NULL CHECK (

294	    ),

296	    final_payload_json TEXT,

300	    created_at REAL NOT NULL,

307	    ON decision_records(workspace_id, curator_action);
308	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_decision_records_ws_created

318	class DecisionRecordStore:
319	    """Per-workspace SQLite-backed decision telemetry store.
320	
321	    Same per-call WAL-connection pattern as M-21 / M-D7 / M-D10 —
322	    each public method opens its own connection, executes its
323	    transaction, and closes. Avoids cross-thread connection sharing
324	    issues.
325	
326	    Schema is idempotent; multiple `__init__` calls on the same
327	    `db_path` are safe. The schema also coexists with M-21
328	    workspace memory + M-D7 retrieval cache + M-D10 freshness
329	    alerts in the same file (different table names; no FK cross-
330	    references).
331	    """
332	
333	    def __init__(self, db_path: Path) -> None:
334	        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
335	        self._db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
336	        self._init_schema()
337	
338	    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
339	        conn = sqlite3.connect(
340	            str(self._db_path),
341	            isolation_level=None,
342	            detect_types=sqlite3.PARSE_DECLTYPES,
343	        )
344	        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
345	        conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
346	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
347	        return conn
348	
349	    def _init_schema(self) -> None:
350	        conn = self._connect()
351	        try:
352	            conn.executescript(_SCHEMA)
353	        finally:
354	            conn.close()
355	
356	    def record_decision(
357	        self,
358	        *,
359	        workspace_id: str,
360	        decision_kind: DecisionKind,
361	        query: str,
362	        proposed_payload: dict[str, Any],
363	        proposed_confidence: float,
364	        clock: float | None = None,
365	    ) -> DecisionRecord:
366	        """Record a fresh decision in PENDING state.
367	
368	        Args:
369	          workspace_id: scope tenancy.
370	          decision_kind: induction | scope_gate.
371	          query: free-text user query.
372	          proposed_payload: JSON-serializable system proposal.
373	          proposed_confidence: in [0, 1].
374	          clock: optional override for tests.
375	
376	        Returns:
377	          The newly-recorded DecisionRecord (curator_action=PENDING,
378	          decided_at=None, final_payload=None, diff_payload=None).
379	
380	        Raises:
381	          DecisionTelemetryError: invalid confidence, unserializable
382	          payload, or empty workspace_id / query.
383	        """
384	        if not workspace_id:
385	            raise DecisionTelemetryError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
386	        if not query:
387	            raise DecisionTelemetryError("query must be non-empty")
388	        if not isinstance(decision_kind, DecisionKind):
389	            raise DecisionTelemetryError(
390	                f"decision_kind must be DecisionKind, got "
391	                f"{type(decision_kind).__name__}"
392	            )
393	        if not 0.0 <= proposed_confidence <= 1.0:
394	            raise DecisionTelemetryError(
395	                f"proposed_confidence {proposed_confidence} outside [0, 1]"
396	            )
397	        try:
398	            payload_json = json.dumps(proposed_payload, sort_keys=True)
399	        except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
400	            raise DecisionTelemetryError(
401	                f"proposed_payload not JSON-serializable: {exc}"
402	            ) from exc
403	
404	        record_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
405	        now = clock if clock is not None else time.time()
406	        conn = self._connect()
407	        try:
408	            conn.execute(
409	                """
410	                INSERT INTO decision_records (
411	                    record_id, workspace_id, decision_kind, query,
412	                    proposed_payload_json, proposed_confidence,
413	                    curator_action, final_payload_json, diff_payload_json,
414	                    actor_user_id, notes, created_at, decided_at
415	                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, ?, NULL)
416	                """,
417	                (
418	                    record_id,
419	                    workspace_id,
420	                    decision_kind.value,
421	                    query,
422	                    payload_json,
423	                    proposed_confidence,
424	                    CuratorAction.PENDING.value,
425	                    now,
426	                ),
427	            )
428	        finally:
429	            conn.close()
430	
431	        return DecisionRecord(
432	            record_id=record_id,
433	            workspace_id=workspace_id,
434	            decision_kind=decision_kind,
435	            query=query,
436	            proposed_payload=proposed_payload,
437	            proposed_confidence=proposed_confidence,
438	            curator_action=CuratorAction.PENDING,
439	            final_payload=None,
440	            diff_payload=None,
441	            actor_user_id=None,
442	            notes=None,
443	            created_at=now,
444	            decided_at=None,
445	        )
446	
447	    def update_curator_action(
448	        self,
449	        record_id: str,
450	        *,
451	        workspace_id: str,
452	        curator_action: CuratorAction,
453	        actor_user_id: str,
454	        final_payload: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
455	        diff_payload: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
456	        notes: str | None = None,
457	        clock: float | None = None,
458	    ) -> DecisionRecord:
459	        """Transition a PENDING record to a terminal curator action.
460	
461	        Args:
462	          record_id: record to update.
463	          workspace_id: scoping field. v2: required, matched against
464	            the row's stored workspace_id. Raises
465	            DecisionTelemetryStateError if (record_id, workspace_id)
466	            does not exist — protecting against cross-workspace
467	            transitions even if a caller knows another workspace's
468	            record_id.
469	          curator_action: terminal action (NOT pending).
470	          actor_user_id: required for terminal actions.
471	          final_payload: required for accepted_as_proposed / modified /
472	            overridden; must be None for rejected.
473	          diff_payload: optional structural diff. Must be None for
474	            accepted_as_proposed and rejected; expected for
475	            modified / overridden.
476	          notes: optional curator rationale.
477	          clock: optional override for tests.
478	
479	        Raises:
480	          DecisionTelemetryStateError: record not found in workspace,
481	            already terminal, invalid transition.
482	          DecisionTelemetryError: validation failure on
483	            curator_action / final_payload / diff_payload (see
484	            _validate_terminal_args).
485	        """
486	        if not workspace_id:
487	            raise DecisionTelemetryError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
488	        # All cross-action invariants centralized in
489	        # _validate_terminal_args (Codex round-1 MED fix v2).
490	        _validate_terminal_args(
491	            curator_action, actor_user_id, final_payload, diff_payload,
492	        )
493	
494	        try:
495	            final_json = (
496	                json.dumps(final_payload, sort_keys=True)
497	                if final_payload is not None
498	                else None
499	            )
500	            diff_json = (
501	                json.dumps(diff_payload, sort_keys=True)
502	                if diff_payload is not None
503	                else None
504	            )
505	        except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
506	            raise DecisionTelemetryError(
507	                f"payload not JSON-serializable: {exc}"
508	            ) from exc
509	
510	        now = clock if clock is not None else time.time()
511	        conn = self._connect()
512	        try:
513	            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
514	            row = conn.execute(
515	                """
516	                SELECT curator_action FROM decision_records
517	                 WHERE record_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?
518	                """,
519	                (record_id, workspace_id),
520	            ).fetchone()
521	            if row is None:
522	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
523	                raise DecisionTelemetryStateError(
524	                    f"record_id {record_id!r} not found in workspace "
525	                    f"{workspace_id!r}"
526	                )
527	            existing_action = CuratorAction(row["curator_action"])
528	            if existing_action in _TERMINAL_ACTIONS:
529	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
530	                raise DecisionTelemetryStateError(
531	                    f"record_id {record_id!r} already terminal "
532	                    f"({existing_action.value}); transitions are "
533	                    f"forbidden — record a new decision instead"
534	                )
535	            conn.execute(
536	                """
537	                UPDATE decision_records
538	                   SET curator_action = ?,
539	                       actor_user_id = ?,
540	                       final_payload_json = ?,
541	                       diff_payload_json = ?,
542	                       notes = ?,
543	                       decided_at = ?
544	                 WHERE record_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?
545	                """,
546	                (
547	                    curator_action.value,
548	                    actor_user_id,
549	                    final_json,
550	                    diff_json,
551	                    notes,
552	                    now,
553	                    record_id,
554	                    workspace_id,
555	                ),
556	            )
557	            conn.execute("COMMIT")
558	        except DecisionTelemetryError:
559	            raise
560	        except Exception:
561	            conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
562	            raise
563	        finally:
564	            conn.close()
565	
566	        return self._must_get(record_id, workspace_id)
567	
568	    def get(
569	        self, record_id: str, *, workspace_id: str,
570	    ) -> DecisionRecord | None:
571	        """Fetch a record by id within the given workspace.
572	
573	        Codex round-1 MED fix (v2): `workspace_id` is required and
574	        the query filters on both columns. Without this, a caller
575	        with a record_id from another workspace could read across
576	        workspace boundaries — even though record_id is a UUID4,
577	        the M-D7 / M-D10 pattern is to require workspace_id
578	        explicitly so cross-workspace access is never possible
579	        through the public API.
580	
581	        Returns None if no record matches `(record_id, workspace_id)`.
582	        Raises DecisionTelemetryError on empty workspace_id.
583	        """
584	        if not workspace_id:
585	            raise DecisionTelemetryError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
586	        conn = self._connect()
587	        try:
588	            row = conn.execute(
589	                """
590	                SELECT * FROM decision_records
591	                 WHERE record_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?
592	                """,
593	                (record_id, workspace_id),
594	            ).fetchone()
595	            return _row_to_record(row) if row is not None else None
596	        finally:
597	            conn.close()
598	
599	    def _must_get(
600	        self, record_id: str, workspace_id: str,
601	    ) -> DecisionRecord:
602	        record = self.get(record_id, workspace_id=workspace_id)
603	        if record is None:
604	            raise DecisionTelemetryStateError(
605	                f"record_id {record_id!r} not found post-update "
606	                f"(workspace {workspace_id!r})"
607	            )
608	        return record
609	
610	    def list_for_workspace(
611	        self,
612	        workspace_id: str,
613	        *,
614	        decision_kind: DecisionKind | None = None,
615	        curator_action: CuratorAction | None = None,
616	        limit: int | None = None,
617	    ) -> list[DecisionRecord]:
618	        """List records for a workspace, optionally filtered.
619	
620	        Records returned ordered by `created_at DESC` (most recent first).
621	        `limit` caps the result count.
622	        """
623	        if not workspace_id:
624	            raise DecisionTelemetryError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
625	        clauses = ["workspace_id = ?"]
626	        params: list[Any] = [workspace_id]
627	        if decision_kind is not None:
628	            clauses.append("decision_kind = ?")
629	            params.append(decision_kind.value)
630	        if curator_action is not None:
631	            clauses.append("curator_action = ?")
632	            params.append(curator_action.value)
633	        sql = (
634	            "SELECT * FROM decision_records "
635	            f"WHERE {' AND '.join(clauses)} "
636	            "ORDER BY created_at DESC"
637	        )
638	        if limit is not None:
639	            if limit < 0:
640	                raise DecisionTelemetryError("limit must be non-negative")
641	            sql += f" LIMIT {int(limit)}"
642	
643	        conn = self._connect()
644	        try:
645	            return [
646	                _row_to_record(row)
647	                for row in conn.execute(sql, params).fetchall()
648	            ]
649	        finally:
650	            conn.close()
651	
652	    def count_for_workspace(
653	        self,
654	        workspace_id: str,
655	        *,
656	        decision_kind: DecisionKind | None = None,
657	        curator_action: CuratorAction | None = None,
658	    ) -> int:
659	        """Count matching records — cheap aggregate without
660	        materializing the full list. Useful for M-D4 calibration
661	        queries (e.g. 'how many accepted_as_proposed inductions for
662	        this workspace over the last 6mo')."""
663	        if not workspace_id:
664	            raise DecisionTelemetryError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
665	        clauses = ["workspace_id = ?"]
666	        params: list[Any] = [workspace_id]
667	        if decision_kind is not None:
668	            clauses.append("decision_kind = ?")
669	            params.append(decision_kind.value)
670	        if curator_action is not None:
671	            clauses.append("curator_action = ?")
672	            params.append(curator_action.value)
673	        sql = (
674	            f"SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM decision_records "
675	            f"WHERE {' AND '.join(clauses)}"
676	        )
677	        conn = self._connect()
678	        try:
679	            row = conn.execute(sql, params).fetchone()
680	            return int(row["c"])
681	        finally:
682	            conn.close()
683	
684	
685	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
686	# Helpers
687	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
688	
689	
690	def _row_to_record(row: sqlite3.Row) -> DecisionRecord:
691	    proposed = json.loads(row["proposed_payload_json"])
692	    final_raw = row["final_payload_json"]
693	    final = json.loads(final_raw) if final_raw is not None else None
694	    diff_raw = row["diff_payload_json"]
695	    diff = json.loads(diff_raw) if diff_raw is not None else None
696	    return DecisionRecord(
697	        record_id=row["record_id"],
698	        workspace_id=row["workspace_id"],
699	        decision_kind=DecisionKind(row["decision_kind"]),
700	        query=row["query"],
701	        proposed_payload=proposed,
702	        proposed_confidence=float(row["proposed_confidence"]),
703	        curator_action=CuratorAction(row["curator_action"]),
704	        final_payload=final,
705	        diff_payload=diff,
706	        actor_user_id=row["actor_user_id"],
707	        notes=row["notes"],
708	        created_at=float(row["created_at"]),
709	        decided_at=(
710	            float(row["decided_at"]) if row["decided_at"] is not None else None
711	        ),
712	    )
713	
714	
715	__all__ = [
716	    "CuratorAction",
717	    "DecisionKind",
718	    "DecisionRecord",
719	    "DecisionRecordStore",

1	"""M-D6 phase 1 v1 (Phase D): Cross-domain template routing substrate.
2	
3	M-D5 phase 2 (`scope_classifier_llm.py`) produces a
4	`ScopeClassification` with a `domain` tag. M-D6 phase 1 ships
5	the **cross-domain routing substrate**: given a domain tag,
6	look up the matching `DomainTemplate` and route to the
7	right `DomainAdapter` (HTTP client / retrieval backend).
8	
9	Phase 1 v1 ships substrate ONLY:
10	  - `DomainTemplate` dataclass — ID + display name + tier
11	    expectations + scope-template path
12	  - `DomainTemplateRegistry` — workspace-scoped lookup
13	  - `DomainAdapter` Protocol — pluggable retrieval seam
14	  - `route_to_domain(classification, registry, adapters)` —
15	    pure orchestrator
16	
17	Phase 2 (deferred) ships concrete `DomainAdapter` impls:
18	  - NIST / MITRE for cybersec
19	  - FAERS / EudraVigilance for pharmacovigilance
20	  - ASTM / NIST for materials
21	
22	This separation keeps the routing logic testable without API
23	costs and lets adapter impls land independently as their HTTP
24	contracts firm up.
25	
26	## Substrate boundary
27	
28	Imports `scope_classifier` (phase 1 contracts) +
29	`scope_classifier_llm` (phase 2 verdict shape) + stdlib only.
30	No HTTP, no DB, no LLM clients.
31	
32	See `docs/md6_phase1_threat_model.md` for boundaries.
33	"""
34	
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
38	from enum import Enum
39	from typing import Mapping, Protocol
40	
41	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.scope_classifier import (
42	    ScopeClassification,
43	    ScopeClassifierError,
44	    ScopeVerdict,
45	)
46	
47	
48	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49	# Errors
50	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	
52	
53	class DomainRouterError(ScopeClassifierError):
54	    """Raised on contract violations — unknown domain, missing
55	    adapter, malformed registry."""
56	
57	
58	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
59	# Domain template + registry
60	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	
62	
63	@dataclass(frozen=True)
64	class DomainTemplate:
65	    """One domain's routing manifest.
66	
67	    `domain_id`: stable string ID (e.g. "clinical", "policy").
68	    Matches the `domain` field on a `ScopeClassification`.
69	
70	    `display_name`: human-readable label for UI / dashboards.
71	
72	    `scope_template_path`: relative path to the YAML scope
73	    template (e.g. `config/scope_templates/clinical.yaml`).
74	    Substrate doesn't load the YAML — that's caller territory.
75	
76	    `expected_adapter_ids`: tuple of `DomainAdapter` IDs that
77	    this domain requires for retrieval. Routing fails if any
78	    expected adapter is missing from the supplied adapters
79	    dict at `route_to_domain` time.
80	    """
81	
82	    domain_id: str
83	    display_name: str
84	    scope_template_path: str
85	    expected_adapter_ids: tuple[str, ...] = field(default_factory=tuple)
86	
87	
88	class DomainTemplateRegistry:
89	    """Workspace-scoped registry of domain templates.
90	
91	    Substrate-only — no DB persistence. Caller constructs with
92	    a tuple of templates; routing reads from this registry.
93	
94	    Workspace scoping is achieved by callers maintaining one
95	    registry per workspace; the substrate doesn't enforce
96	    workspace_id directly (consistent with how M-D5 phase 1
97	    composes with phase 1 store via per-call workspace_id).
98	    """
99	
100	    def __init__(self, templates: tuple[DomainTemplate, ...]) -> None:
101	        if not isinstance(templates, tuple):
102	            raise DomainRouterError(
103	                f"templates must be a tuple of DomainTemplate, got "
104	                f"{type(templates).__name__}"
105	            )
106	        ids: dict[str, DomainTemplate] = {}
107	        for i, tpl in enumerate(templates):
108	            if not isinstance(tpl, DomainTemplate):
109	                raise DomainRouterError(
110	                    f"templates[{i}] must be DomainTemplate, got "
111	                    f"{type(tpl).__name__}"
112	                )
113	            # Codex round-1 MEDIUM fix (v2): type-check domain_id.
114	            # v1 only checked truthiness — DomainTemplate(domain_id=123)
115	            # was accepted at construction even though get()/has()
116	            # only operate on str ids, so malformed manifests
117	            # weren't rejected at the documented boundary.
118	            if not isinstance(tpl.domain_id, str):
119	                raise DomainRouterError(
120	                    f"templates[{i}].domain_id must be str, got "
121	                    f"{type(tpl.domain_id).__name__}"
122	                )
123	            if not tpl.domain_id:
124	                raise DomainRouterError(
125	                    f"templates[{i}].domain_id must be non-empty"
126	                )
127	            # Codex round-2 MEDIUM fix (v3): type-check
128	            # expected_adapter_ids tuple. v2 trusted the
129	            # runtime annotation; `expected_adapter_ids=(123,)`
130	            # constructed successfully and later degraded into
131	            # MISSING_ADAPTERS at route time instead of failing
132	            # at registry construction.
133	            if not isinstance(tpl.expected_adapter_ids, tuple):
134	                raise DomainRouterError(
135	                    f"templates[{i}].expected_adapter_ids must be "
136	                    f"tuple, got "
137	                    f"{type(tpl.expected_adapter_ids).__name__}"
138	                )
139	            for j, aid in enumerate(tpl.expected_adapter_ids):
140	                if not isinstance(aid, str):
141	                    raise DomainRouterError(
142	                        f"templates[{i}].expected_adapter_ids[{j}] "
143	                        f"must be str, got {type(aid).__name__}"
144	                    )
145	                if not aid:
146	                    raise DomainRouterError(
147	                        f"templates[{i}].expected_adapter_ids[{j}] "
148	                        "must be non-empty"
149	                    )
150	            if tpl.domain_id in ids:
151	                raise DomainRouterError(
152	                    f"duplicate domain_id {tpl.domain_id!r} in templates"
153	                )
154	            ids[tpl.domain_id] = tpl
155	        self._by_id = ids
156	
157	    @property
158	    def domain_ids(self) -> tuple[str, ...]:
159	        """Stable-sorted tuple of registered domain IDs."""
160	        return tuple(sorted(self._by_id.keys()))
161	
162	    def get(self, domain_id: str) -> DomainTemplate:
163	        """Lookup by domain_id. Raises if missing."""
164	        if not isinstance(domain_id, str):
165	            raise DomainRouterError(
166	                f"domain_id must be str, got {type(domain_id).__name__}"
167	            )
168	        try:
169	            return self._by_id[domain_id]
170	        except KeyError:
171	            raise DomainRouterError(
172	                f"unknown domain_id {domain_id!r}; "
173	                f"registered: {self.domain_ids}"
174	            )
175	
176	    def has(self, domain_id: str) -> bool:
177	        return isinstance(domain_id, str) and domain_id in self._by_id
178

211	class RoutingOutcome(str, Enum):
212	    """Per-route outcome.

214	    ROUTED: classifier returned IN_SCOPE with a known domain;
215	       all expected adapters present.
216	    REJECTED_OUT_OF_SCOPE: classifier returned OUT_OF_SCOPE.

226	    REJECTED_UNCERTAIN = "rejected_uncertain"
227	    UNKNOWN_DOMAIN = "unknown_domain"
228	    MISSING_ADAPTERS = "missing_adapters"

247	    rationale: str
